segments.s659-g579: چرا 'why' opens a rhetorical question; باید is the modal/obligatory auxiliary 'ought/should'. The noun phrase از خون درویش گنج = 'treasure from the blood of the dervish' uses از to mark source ('from the blood of...') and places the possessor (درویش) after خون.
segments.s659-g580: که introduces a purposive/result clause ('that/so that'). او 'he' is the beneficiary or agent implied; شاد باشد uses شاد (happy) with باشد (present subjunctive/optative of بودن) meaning 'may be / might be happy'. تن و جان 'body and soul' is a fixed pair and به رنج means 'in/with suffering' (به + noun showing state), so the clause says 'that he be happy while body and soul are in suffering.'
segments.s659-g581: ازان is the archaic contraction of از آن ('to/from that'); here it begins a noun phrase 'to that person'. کس که بستد is a relative clause 'the person who bound/entrusted' with بستد (past of بستن) as the verb. بدو is the dative clitic 'to him' and باز‌ده is the command 'give back' (باز + ده, imperative). The whole line = 'Return (it) to that person who entrusted it to him.'
segments.s659-g582: ازان پس = 'after that / then'. به مرو is a prepositional phrase 'to Merv' (مرو = the city Merv). اندر آواز ده is an instruction: اندر 'in/there' + آواز ده (imperative 'give a voice' = 'proclaim/announce'); together 'then go to Merv and announce it there.'
segments.s659-g583: بفرمای here functions as the reporting/command verb ('he ordered' / 'command'), used narratively. داری زدن بر درش: زد ن بر در means 'to strike/beat upon the door'; درش is در + enclitic ش 'his/its' (door of him). داری is a verbal/auxiliary form indicating the action to be carried out (here best understood as marking the executing of the action), so the phrase issues the order 'have his door struck' or 'strike his door.'
segments.s659-g584: به بیداری = 'for/for the purpose of awakening' (به with an abstract noun). کشور و لشکرش lists the targets: 'the country/realm and his army' with لشکرش the noun 'army' plus enclitic 'his'. Together this gives the reason/purpose of the order: 'to awaken the realm and his army.'
segments.s659-g33: دژم (predicate adjective) precedes the verb and describes the subject's mood; گشت is a past-tense/resultative verb 'became/turned' (3rd singular). زان is a causal preposition 'from/ because of that' linking the reason phrase to the verb, and کار (here in the sense 'deed') names the thing that caused the change; بوزرجمهر is the proper name ('Bozorgmehr') attached to کار by simple juxtaposition (classical Persian often omits an ezāfe).
segments.s659-g34: فروماند is a past/resultant verbal adjective meaning 'was dejected/worn out' and functions as the predicate; از introduces the agent/cause ('because of' / 'by'); کارگردان is a noun 'director/manager' and سپهر 'sky/heavens' follows it as its domain (i.e. 'director of the heavens'). Word order is cause/agent after the verb.
segments.s659-g35: بدانست is a past-tense verb 'he knew/he perceived'. کآمد is a fused relative-like clause 'that which came' or 'what would come' introducing the content of his knowledge; بتنگی ('narrowness/tightness') and نشیب ('descent/low point') form a noun phrase describing the adverse change expected — the order is noun modifier (بتنگی) then noun (نشیب).
segments.s659-g36: زمانه is the subject ('time/fortune'); بگیرد is a present/subjunctive-style verb 'takes/will take' (classical Persian uses this form for general/potential actions). فریب and نهیب are the objects joined by و ('and'), meaning 'deception and rebuke' — i.e. the things the time will 'take' or be afflicted by.
segments.s659-g37: چوبیدارشد is a compound verbal phrase meaning literally 'became stick-bearing' — i.e. the king took up a staff/took a threatening posture (past tense, 3rd sing.). شاه is the subject; و links to the next clause; او را uses the object marker را to mark 'him' as the definite object; بدید is the past tense 'saw'.
segments.s659-g38: کزان سان is a short adverbial phrase 'from which/at that (event) thus' (کزان = 'from which'; سان = 'so/thus'). همی is an older emphatic/progressive particle adding immediacy. لب بدندان گزید is an idiomatic verb phrase 'bit his lip (between his teeth)' — لب (lip) + بدندان ('with the teeth') + گزید (past of 'to bite'), expressing anger or restraint.
segments.s659-g39: گمانی is a noun 'a thought/idea' (here 'he entertained a thought'); چنان modifies it 'such'; برد is the past verb 'took/held' used idiomatically 'he had/harbored (such) a thought'. کو را is an archaic connective (roughly 'that he' / 'whom' with را as object marker), and بخواب is the infinitive or purpose phrase 'to sleep'/'to put to sleep' (here meaning 'to silence/kill/put to sleep').
segments.s659-g40: خورش is a noun 'food/dish' used metaphorically 'his food'; کرد is a causative/lexical verb 'made/caused' (i.e. 'made him food'). بر + پرورش is a prepositional phrase 'for/upon upbringing' (بر marking purpose/benefit), and برشتاب is an adverbial/verb form 'hastened/quickly' indicating prompt action.
segments.s659-g305: همان is a demonstrative here ('that' / 'the said'), followed by بخرد used substantively as 'the wise one'. و simply connects the two nouns. موبد راه‌جوی is a noun plus a postpositive modifier: موبد 'mobed/priest' modified by راه‌جوی 'seeker of the way' (classical Persian often places modifiers after the head noun). The whole phrase is the subject/topic for the verb in the next segment.
segments.s659-g306: گو is an imperative/hortative 'say' (introducing reported speech). بر here functions as a prepositional particle meaning roughly 'about'/'on' and governs منش ('origin/whence'). کو (interrogative 'where') with بود (past copula 'was') forms an embedded interrogative 'where ... was', and شاه جوی is a compound noun 'one who seeks the kingship' that links to منش (i.e. 'the origin from which the king-seeker was'). Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect: 'منش کو بود شاه جوی' = 'from where the king-seeker was'.
segments.s659-g307: همه پاک is a nominal phrase 'all (those) pure' (همه + adjective used substantively). در is the preposition 'in', بارگاه is 'the royal court', and تواند is a modal verb 'can/may' (third-person singular present/optative) expressing permission or ability. The verb refers to entering the court; Persian often uses a singular-form verb with collective subjects in poetic register.
segments.s659-g308: وگر is the conditional 'if' ('but if'), introducing the subordinate clause. در جهان 'in the world' sets the domain, نیک‌خواه is a noun 'well-wisher' (i.e. a benevolent person). تواند repeats the modal 'may/can'; the verb's object (entering the barگاه) is omitted here by ellipsis and is supplied from the previous clause.
segments.s659-g309: همین درج 'this very inscription' (همین emphasizing identity). با introduces an instrumental list 'with lock and مهر and نشان'—قفل 'lock', مهر here 'seal/stamp' (not 'love' in this context), نشان 'sign/token'. The prepositional phrase qualifies درج as being marked/sealed.
segments.s659-g310: ببینند is a present/subjunctive form 'they see' or a general 'are seeing' used here to state who perceives the inscription. بیدار دل is a compound adjective-noun 'wakeful/alert-hearted' modifying سرکشان; together they form the subject/agent group 'the alert-hearted, the rebellious' who perceive the inscription. Word order places the verb before its subject for emphasis (poetic inversion).
segments.s659-g311: بگویند here functions as a hortative/imperative 'let them say' (a request to speak), روشن is an adverb/adjective 'clearly/plainly'. که introduces a subordinate clause 'that/which', زیر نهفت is a verbless verblike phrase 'hidden beneath' (زیر 'under' + نهفت 'hidden/concealed'). The line asks for a clear statement of what is concealed.
segments.s659-g312: چه چیزست is an interrogative/existential 'what is it' (چِه + چیزست = 'what thing is it'). وآن 'and that' refers back to the hidden thing just named. با خرد هست جفت is a predicative phrase 'is paired with wisdom' (با = 'with', خرد = 'wisdom', هست = copula 'is', جفت = 'pair/match'); word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect but reads 'and that is a match/paired with wisdom.'
segments.s659-g465: بیاویز is an imperative ('hang!' or 'suspend!') addressed to someone; پایش is the object with the possessive suffix -ش = 'his foot'. ز + دار بلند is a prepositional/ablative phrase 'from (the) high gallows' placed after the object. Word order here is Verb + object(+possessive) + origin/adjunct.
segments.s659-g466: بدان here functions as a connective/adverbial ('thereby/so' / 'know this') leading into a purpose clause introduced by تا. تا introduces a subjunctive/purpose clause; بازگردد is third-person singular subjunctive (or optative) 'may/let it return', and بدو is the indirect object 'to him'. گزند is the noun 'harm' that is the subject/affected thing of بازگردد.
segments.s659-g467: که begins a subordinate clause ('that/which'); از کهتران نیز = 'even/from the lesser ones' (کهتران = inferiors/younger men) with نیز 'also/even'. در کارزار is the locative prepositional phrase 'in the battle/battlefield' following the noun phrase. The order is relative particle + origin + adverb + locative.
segments.s659-g468: فزونی is the abstract noun 'superiority/excess'. نجویند is the verbal form meaning 'they seek' (here used in a negative idea introduced by the clause: 'should not seek' or 'do not seek'); its stem is جویدن/جستن with the negative prefix ن-. با شهریار 'with the king' functions as the target/relatum (poetically 'against/over the king' = claiming superiority over the king).
segments.s659-g469: دگر نامداری is a nominative noun phrase 'another renowned one' (دگر = another). ز کارآگهان 'of/the from the informed/experienced' uses ز as a genitival/origin marker 'from/among the informed ones' — it's a brief attributive phrase describing the speaker.
segments.s659-g470: چنین گفت is a fixed narrative formula 'thus he said' (گفت is the past-tense verb 'he said' — here listed senses are lexical but the context is the past-speech verb). کای is the archaic vocative particle 'O' used to address rulers in Shahnameh; شهریار جهان = 'king/lord of the world' is the addressee. Word order: formulaic adverb + verb, then the vocative.
segments.s659-g471: به شبگیر is an adverbial phrase 'at nightfall' (به + شبگیر used temporally). برزین is the proper name (subject). بشد is an archaic past form 'it came about / he went / he set out' (literary past); با سپاه 'with the army' is a comitative adjunct placed after the verb. Overall: temporal phrase + subject + verb + accompaniment.
segments.s659-g472: ستاره‌شناسی here denotes the star-know-er(s) (astrologer/astronomer) used as the subject. بیامد is the simple past 'came'; ز راه 'from/by the road' is an ablative/coming-from phrase. Word order: subject + past verb + origin/route.
segments.s659-g569: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when' introducing the clause. نامش is the noun 'name' plus the enclitic pronoun -ش 'his', with the possessor attached. ز دفتر is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from the book/register' (ز = from, دفتر = book). بخواند is a narrative/past reading of خواندن here meaning 'read aloud' (the verb appears before its subject, a common poetic inversion). دبیر at the end is the subject 'the scribe' placed after the verb for stylistic order.
segments.s659-g570: برد in context is the action 'brought/took' (past of بردن) though that precise verbal sense was not listed in the provided options; the verb heads the clause. پیش کودک = 'before/in front of the child' (پیش = in the presence of / before, کودک = child). درم ناگزیر is a noun phrase 'a dirham (درم) inevitable/compulsory (ناگزیر)' with ناگزیر functioning adjectivally/adverbially to indicate it was given as a necessary/forced payment.
segments.s659-g571: چنین هم is an emphatic adverbial 'thus/likewise'; هم adds an 'also' or confirmatory sense. به سال اندرون چار بار is a temporal adverbial phrase: به سال = 'in/for a year', اندرون = 'within', چار بار = 'four times' (چار = four, بار = times/occurrences). Word order places the time-frame together at the front for emphasis.
segments.s659-g572: مبادا is a negative jussive 'may it not be / lest'; it introduces a fearful wish. که + باشد forms the subordinate clause ('that it be / that it should be'); باشد is subjunctive/optative here ('be'). از این کار خوار is the predicate 'despised because of this deed' (ازین = 'from/of this', کار = 'deed/act', خوار = 'despised/lowly'), with خوار as the predicative adjective.
segments.s659-g573: دگر گفت is a transitional phrase 'then/again he said' (دگر = again/then placed before گفت 'said'); the speaker name انوشه follows as subject. بدی here is a poetic/archaic imperative/optative form meaning 'give' (a contracted/older form of بده/بدهی), governing the following objects 'سال و ماه' = 'a year and a month' (i.e. a year's and a month's pay); the phrase is direct speech introduced by گفت.
segments.s659-g574: به مرو = 'to Merv' (به = to/into, مرو = the city Merv). اندرون here means 'into the inner quarters/household' (an adverbial direction). پهلوان سپاه is a genitive-like compound 'the champion of the host/army' (پهلوان = champion, سپاه = army), with the possessor-noun order typical in Persian poetry (noun + possessor). The whole segment gives the destination/recipient of the payment/order.
segments.s659-g575: فراوان modifies the verb phrase adverbially 'in abundance' or 'many'. درم گرد کرد is a verbal compound meaning 'collected dirhams' (درم = dirhams, گرد کردن = to collect/gather) with کرد as the auxiliary 'did/made' following گرد. و بخورد is a short clause coordinating the next action; بخورد here is contextually a verbal form meaning something like 'and bestowed/distributed' (the precise sense is idiomatic/poetic), linking to the scattering mentioned next.
segments.s659-g576: پراگنده گشتند is an intransitive/perfect construction 'they became scattered' (پراگنده = scattered, گشتند = became/turned — past plural). زان مرز مرد is the source and subject: زان = 'from that', مرز = 'border/frontier', مرد = 'men' — together 'the men of that frontier'. Word order places the verb phrase before the topical source and subject, a common poetic inversion.
segments.s659-g1: چنان بد که is an archaic/poetic clause meaning “it was such that” (چنان بود که), where بد here stands in for بود. The relative conjunction که introduces the following clause. کسری بدان روزگار = “Khosrow at that time”: بدَان is a contracted locative (ب + آن = 'in that'), so the phrase places the king in that time.
segments.s659-g2: برفت is an archaic/perfective past meaning “departed/went forth” (بر + رفت). The verb comes before its complements in this verse line. از مداین gives the origin/place ‘from Madāʾin,’ and ز بهر شکار is a purpose phrase ‘for the sake of hunting’ (ز بهر = 'for').
segments.s659-g3: همی‌تاخت is an iterative/imperfective past/past‑habitual sense here meaning ‘kept charging’ or ‘was charging.’ با marks accompaniment. غرم و آهو lists two items joined by و – here آهو is the animal ‘gazelle’; غرم appears as a proper name/term in the narrative (not the literal sense of the lexicon entries), and به دشت means ‘into/onto the plain’ (به + noun = motion toward).
segments.s659-g4: پراگند شد is a compound verb: پراگند (scattered) + شد (became/was) = ‘was scattered.’ The following clause غرم و او مانده گشت uses the copular/auxiliary گشت (= became/was) after مانده (remaining) to mean ‘he remained/was left behind.’ The coordination with و connects the two short clauses.
segments.s659-g5: ز هامون is a poetic ablative ‘from the Hamun’ (a marsh/plain). بر + مرغزاری marks movement toward the bird‑meadow; reached is expressed by رسید (past ‘arrived/reached’). The verb رسید closes the clause after its place complements.
segments.s659-g6: درخت و گیا دید is a simple coordinate object list ‘he saw trees and plants’ with the verb دید (past ‘saw’) following the objects. و هم سایه دید repeats the verb to emphasize an additional object — هم = ‘also’ — so the line lists what he beheld in parallel structure (object(s) + verb, then object + verb).
segments.s659-g7: همی‌راند is an imperfective/past iterative verb meaning ‘was riding/driving’ (the progressive past sense common in epic diction). با شاه بوزرجمهر is an accompaniment phrase ‘with King Bozorgmehr’ (با + title + name). Word order places the verb before the accompaniment in the verse.
segments.s659-g8: ز بهر پرستش هم از بهر مهر gives two parallel motives: ز بهر + noun = ‘for the sake of …’. Here پرستش = ‘worship’ and مهر = ‘love/affection’; هم ... هم (here condensed) signals ‘both … and …’ or ‘for ... and also for ...’. The prepositions ز/از with بهر are poetic markers of purpose.
segments.s659-g89: چنان هم‌چو + داناش is a fronted manner phrase: چنان هم‌چو = 'thus/like so', داناش is دانا + enclitic -ش ('his wise one' or 'to his sage'), and فرموده بود is the compound past (فرموده = honorific/perfect participle 'said/commanded' + بود as auxiliary) giving a pluperfect sense 'had said/commanded'. The whole segment means 'he had so spoken/commanded to his wise man.'
segments.s659-g90: نه ... و نه is the correlative negative 'neither ... nor ...'. کم کرد ازان نیز = 'made it less from that (amount) also' (i.e. 'reduced it'), and برفزود is a simple past 'increased' or 'added'. Together: 'he neither reduced it from that nor increased it.'
segments.s659-g91: به گفتار دانا is a prepositional phrase 'by/at the speech of the wise (man)' (به + گفتارِ دانا). فرو ریخت is a verb+adverb combination where فرو 'down-' modifies ریخت (pour/fall) in the past tense, and آب (placed after the verb for poetic inversion) is the subject: 'water poured down at the sage's word.'
segments.s659-g92: Again the correlative negative نه ... ونه marks two contrary manners. نرم = 'gently', ونه = 'nor', از + ریختن (the verbal noun) indicates 'in/from the act of falling/pouring', and برشتاب here functions as an adverbial notion 'hastily/with haste' describing the manner of falling: 'neither gently nor hastily.'
segments.s659-g93: بدو گفت is the common inversion 'to him (بدو) he said (گفت)'. شاه‌ای is a vocative 'O king' (شاه + vocative -ای), and فزاینده مهر is an epithet in apposition 'increaser/augmenter of favour' describing the king. The segment is direct address: 'He said to him, "O king, increaser of favour".'
segments.s659-g94: که introduces a reported clause 'that'. The following reported speech is: 'این تو را راگفت بوزرجمهر' — این 'this' as demonstrative, تو را 'to you' (object with accusative marker), راگفت 'said/told' (past), and بوزرجمهر (Bozorgmehr) is the subject. Roughly: 'that Bozorgmehr told you this.'
segments.s659-g95: مرا (here tokenized as 'م' + 'را') is the patient 'me' (object). اندرین دانش = 'in this knowledge' (اندر + این + دانش = 'in/within this learning'), او is the subject 'he', داد is past 'gave', and راه is the thing given 'a way/path' (i.e. guidance). Word order is object + adjunct + subject + verb + object (poetic ordering): 'He gave me a way in this knowledge.'
segments.s659-g96: که introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that'. بیند is a present/subjunctive form 'may see' or 'sees', همی is an emphatic/continuous particle placed before the verb ('indeed/still'), and این جهاندار شاه = 'this sovereign king' (این modifying جهاندار, with شاه in apposition). Together: 'so that this sovereign king may behold it.'
segments.s659-g209: بیاورد is a past narrative verb (classical form with ب-) meaning 'brought'. The coordinated phrase گنجور و اسبی گزین lists two direct objects: گنجور (here 'treasure-hoard' / 'treasury') + اسبی گزین ('a chosen/selected horse'), with گزین functioning adjectivally ('chosen') after اسبی. Word order is Verb + object(s), typical of epic Persian.
segments.s659-g210: This clause uses a causative past (کردند, 'they made/caused') with the masdar/nominal action نشست ('sitting/mounting') and the patient شهنشاه ('the shah') before the verb; زین ('saddle') names the place/instrument. Together the sequence means 'they caused the shah to sit/mount on the saddle' — an order of constituents common in classical Persian where the masdar+object precede the causative verb.
segments.s659-g211: به نزدیک دانا functions as an indirect-object/locator phrase 'to/for the wise man nearby' with به + noun; فرستاد is the past tense 'sent' and و گفت ('and said') links to the reported speech clause introduced next. Subject (the shah) is omitted, as is normal in Persian narrative.
segments.s659-g212: The first که introduces reported speech. رنجی که دیدی is a noun + relative clause ('the suffering that you saw') with the relative كه immediately following the head noun. نشاید is an impersonal/modal present meaning 'it is not fitting/ought not,' followed by the (archaising) masdar/verb نهفت ('to hide' / 'be concealed'), so the literal structure is 'it is not fitting that (it) be hidden.'
segments.s659-g213: چنین Rاند is a short clause: چنین ('thus') + راند, a past verb meaning 'rode/drove' (classical epic usage). The prepositional phrase بر سر سپهر بلند ('upon the head/summit of the lofty sky') places the action; note noun + adjective order (سپهر بلند = 'lofty sky').
segments.s659-g214: The second که again introduces a subordinate clause explaining purpose or result. آید 'comes/befalls' is general/presentive ('may come' / 'will come'), and ز ما بر تو چندی گزند literally 'some harm (چندی گزند) from us (ز ما) upon you (بر تو)'. Word order here is typical: verb in the middle with agents/recipients expressed by short prepositions.
segments.s659-g215: This clause is possessive + effect: زیان تو ('your harm/loss') is the cause, مغز مرا ('my brain/mind' with the object clitic marked by را) is the patient, کرد 'made' is the past verb, and تیز 'sharp' is the predicative complement. Note clitic object marking (مرا/را) attached to the noun phrase in classical spelling.
segments.s659-g216: همی is a habitual/continuous adverb ('continually, always') modifying the past verb کردی ('you did'). با تن خویش ('with your own body') uses the possessive compound خوی + ش (here split in the word list), and ستیز ('strife/war') is the noun that follows کردی as the result: 'you made/kept up strife with your own body.' Word order places the adverb and the prepositional phrase before the verb, as is common.
segments.s659-g553: بنالد همی is a present/continuous lamentative: بنالد (from نالیدن) + the old continuous particle همی = 'keeps lamenting'. کاین introduces a subordinate clause ('that/this') and فollows with the noun and passive verb: درم خورده شد is a passive past—خورده (past participle) + شد (auxiliary) = 'was eaten/spent'. The whole segment = 'he kept lamenting that this dirham was spent.'
segments.s659-g554: This is a short clause with a prepositional phrase first: بر او = 'on/for him' (indicating whose account or concern). مهتر و کهتر = 'the greater and the lesser' (two coordinated subjects). آزرده شد is a stative/resultative past (آزرده + شد = 'became distressed'). In Persian poetry the verb may appear singular even with a coordinated subject; the phrase means 'both high and low were distressed on his account.'
segments.s659-g555: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. آگاه شد is the past tense 'became aware/learned' (آگاه + شد). زان سخن شهریار = 'of that speech/word of the king' (زان = 'from/that'), so the segment reads 'when he became aware of that speech of the king.'
segments.s659-g556: که introduces the content-clause ('that/which'): موبد (subject) درم (object) خواست (past) = 'the mobed asked/requested a dirham'. از کاردار ('from the steward') is a prepositional phrase marking source/agent: درم خواست از کاردار = 'asked the steward for a dirham.' Word order here is object before verb and a following از‑phrase indicating from whom.
segments.s659-g557: چنین گفت = 'he said thus' (چنین = 'thus'). The clause that follows uses کز (archaic form of که/which) + خورده = 'that which has been eaten/spent'. منمای is an archaic negative imperative (م + نما + ی) meaning 'do not show/me' or 'do not make (me) see'—here 'do not cause me'. رنج is the object 'suffering/grief'. The whole clause is an admonition: 'do not cause me grief over what has been spent.'
segments.s659-g558: ببخشید here is an imperative meaning 'bestow/give' (not 'forgive' in this context). The direct object is چیزی 'something'. مر is an older enclitic pronoun meaning 'for me' / 'on my account' (beneficiary), while او را is the recipient marked by the object marker را ('to him'). ز گنج = 'from the treasure' indicates source. Word order is poetic: 'Give something, for me, to him, from the treasury.'
segments.s659-g559: دگر گفت = 'another said' or 'someone else said' (دگر = 'another'). جنگی‌سواری is the compound 'war‑riding / a war-ride / cavalry action' (here the activity he undertook). بخست is a past/telic form from بستَن with the prefix ب‑ (poetic), here meaning 'he took up / set about / bound/fastened' in the sense 'set out for' or 'mounted for' (the listed sense 'shut/closed' is a more literal gloss; context gives the sense of beginning the war-ride). The segment reports someone else's account: 'another said he took up a war-ride.'
segments.s659-g560: بدان is a fused/poetic form ('ب' prefix + دان/آن) meaning 'because of/with that' or 'from that'. خستگی = 'fatigue/weariness'. دیر ماند = 'he remained long' (دیر = 'long (of time)', ماند = 'remained/lingered'). و برست = 'and he departed/ceased to be' (برست here indicates removal/departure = 'perished/died'). The clause links the previous action to its consequence: 'because of that fatigue he lingered long and then died.'
segments.s659-g129: این جملهٔ امری را با حرف «که» آغاز می‌کند که در نقل قول‌های دستوری می‌آید («بگویش که ...»). «یک‌بار نزدیک دانا گذار» دستور امری است: «یک‌بار» = once/for a moment, «نزدیک دانا» = the wise man near, و «گذار» فعل امری/طلبی به معنی «قرار بده/بیاور/نزدیک کن» است (شکل کهن/شعری). فعل امری در پایان قرار گرفته که در زبان منظوم و کهن رایج است.
segments.s659-g130: دو فعل امری پشت سر هم آمده‌اند: «ببر» (برو/ببر = take/escort/go) و «بیار» (bring). «زود» قید سرعت است که هر دو فعل را مشخص می‌کند؛ «پیغام» نشانهٔ مفعولِ فعل نخست و «پاسخ» مفعولِ فعل دوم است. ترتیب فعل/قید/مفعول در این ساختار امری طبیعی است.
segments.s659-g131: «بگویش» فعل امری «بگو» با ضمیر مفعولی «ش» (به او) است: «tell him». «که» جملهٔ تابعی می‌آورد و «چون‌بینی» (= «چون + بینی») نقطهٔ زمانی/شرطی است به معنی «وقتی که می‌بیند». «اکنون تنت» یعنی «اکنون تنت/بدنت (را)»؛ در فارسی کهن ترتیب مشخصهٔ فعل/نقطة زمانی/مفعول می‌تواند انعطاف‌پذیر باشد.
segments.s659-g132: «که» جملهٔ دیگری را پیوند می‌دهد: «that ...». «از» در نقش حرف اضافهٔ منبع/عامل به معنی «از/به سبب/بواسطهٔ» است. «میخ تیزست» ترکیب وصفی است: «میخ» (نوا) + «تیزست» = «تیز است» (فعل ترکیبی با «ست» که شکل شعریِ «است» است) به معنی «نخ یا میخ تیز/تیز است». «پیراهنت» = پیراهنِ تو (پیراهن + پسوند مُلکی)، متمم جمله است؛ کل عبارت می‌گوید که پیراهن/لباس او به وسیلهٔ میخی تیز آسیب دیده/سوراخ شده.
segments.s659-g133: «پرستنده آمد» جملهٔ خبری گذشته است: «the worshipper/adorer came». «بداد آن پیام» در این جایگاه معنای فعل‌انبوه/تقلیدیِ «برآمد و آن پیام را داد/رساند» دارد — یعنی آمد تا پیام را برساند یا پیام را داد. این «بداد» شکل کهن و شعری دارد و به معنای «داد/رساند» است (لیست حس‌ها این صورت فعل را نداشت، لذا به‌صورت علامت‌گذاری -1 مشخص شده).
segments.s659-g134: «که بشنید» جملهٔ وابسته است: «that he heard/learned». «زان» = «از آن/از آن پس» به معنای نتیجه‌گیری «from that». «مهر خویش ش کام» ترکیبی است که «مهر» = محبت/عشق، «خوی» بخشِ ابتداییِ «خویش» و «ش» پسوند ملکی «او/ایشان» است؛ «کام» در این‌جا به معنی «انجام/اقتضاء/کمال خواسته» (تحقق آرزو) آمده. ساختار نشان می‌دهد که از آن پیام او شنید که آرزوی محبتش برآورده شده است.
segments.s659-g135: «چنین داد پاسخ» = «thus he gave an answer/thus he replied»؛ «داد» فعل گذشتهٔ «دادن» است. «بمرد جوان» در حقیقت تحریر کهنی از «به مرد جوان» است و به‌معنی «به آن مرد/به جوان» می‌باشد (در متون کهن حرفِ «به» به‌صورت «ب» می‌آید و به اسم می‌چسبد). بنابراین عبارت یعنی «او چنین به جوان پاسخ داد».
segments.s659-g136: جملهٔ نقل‌قولی/توضیحی است: «که روزم به از روز نوشین‌روان». «روزم» این‌جا به‌صورت اسمی = «روز من» است (پسوند ملکی «-م» دارد). ترکیب «به از» در فارسی کهن یک مقایسهٔ برتری است به معنی «بهتر از/برتر از». «روز نوشین‌روان» = «روزِ آنکه دارای روح/دل خوش است»؛ «نوشین‌روان» لقب وصفیِ مرکب است. پس کل عبارت: «که روز من بهتر از روزِ نوشین‌روان است».
segments.s659-g9: فرود آمد is a compound past tense: فرود (descent/alighting) + آمد (came) = 'alighted/descended'. از بارگی is a prepositional phrase 'from/out of the foliage' (بارگی = foliage/leaf) and شاه is the possessor 'the king' (literally 'from the king’s foliage'). نرم follows and functions adverbially ('gently/softly') modifying the verb phrase.
segments.s659-g10: بدان here is a fulcrum phrase: ب (to/into) + دان (etymologically 'to know/consider' but used here in the fixed compound بدَان meaning 'to that/so that'); تاکند is an archaic subjunctive/optative ('that he/they may' or 'so that he may'), introducing the purpose clause. برگیا is a proper name (Bargiyā) acting as the target/agent of the clause, and چشم گرم is a noun+adjective 'gaze/eye warm' = 'ardent/warm gaze' (چشم = gaze/eye, گرم = fervent).
segments.s659-g11: ندید is the past tense with negative prefix ن- + دید (did not see). از پرستندگان is a partitive/genitive phrase 'of the worshippers' (از used here as 'of/from among'), and هیچکس is the indefinite negative 'no one/nobody' serving as the object.
segments.s659-g12: یکی is the numeral/indefinite 'one' or 'a single', خوب رخ is a descriptive compound 'good/beautiful face' (adjective+noun), and ماند is the past 'remained' (subject omitted but understood). با شاه is a prepositional phrase 'with the king', and بس is a short exclamatory/adverbial 'enough' or 'that suffices' concluding the clause.
segments.s659-g13: بغلتید is a past plural verb meaning 'they tumbled/rolled/embraced' (here 'tumbled' or 'rolled about'), چندی is an adverbial 'for a while', and بران is an archaic locative/prepositional 'on/upon that'. مرغزار is the noun 'meadow' acting as the location.
segments.s659-g14: نهاده is the past participle/perfect 'having placed/put' (used as a verb here), سرش is 'his head' (سر + enclitic ـش = his), مهربان is an epithet 'kind/merciful' (applied to the person performing the action), and برکنار is an adverb/adverbial phrase 'aside' or 'to the side', so together the phrase means 'he placed/laid his head aside, the kind one.'
segments.s659-g15: همیشه is a temporal adverb 'always'. ببازوی is the fused/prepositional phrase به + بازوی ('to/on the arm'), آن شاه is 'that king' (demonstrative + noun), and the final بر is the preposition 'on/upon' that links into the next noun phrase describing what is on the arm.
segments.s659-g16: یکی is the numeral 'one/a single', بند بازو is a compound meaning 'arm-band' or 'band for the arm' (بند = band/link, بازو = arm). بدی in the text appears as a lexical item not matching the immediate meaning here (the intended sense is likely an adjectival or verbal form), so it is unclear in the provided senses; پرگهر is an adjective 'full of jewels/jeweled' modifying the band. The whole phrase describes a single jewelled armlet worn on the king's arm.
segments.s659-g353: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; آرد is a classical third-person singular present (or historical present) of آوردن 'bring/bring about.' بد و نیک رای is a noun phrase 'bad and good counsel' where the adjectives precede رای ('counsel/plan') and سپهر ('the heavens') follows as the possessor: 'the counsel of the heavens.' Word order is typical older Persian (conjunction + verb + predicate noun phrase).
segments.s659-g354: This is a correlative listing using repeated چه in the sense 'whether/whatever (one is)'; the three items are coordinated: 'whether king and whether mōbed and whether Bozorgmehr.' و is the normal coordinating 'and.' The sequence emphasizes that the preceding clause (the heavens' counsel) applies to all these figures.
segments.s659-g355: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' (indicating source/origin). تخمی is 'seed' (here: the seed/destiny); که introduces a relative clause 'that/which.' یزدان is the subject 'God' and به اختر gives the locative 'on/in the star.' بکشت is an archaic verbal form (perfect/past or a classical formative) meaning 'caused to be/placed/planted.'
segments.s659-g356: ببایدش is the modal expression 'it must be / one ought' with the enclitic -ش = 'it' attached; بر is the spatial preposition 'upon/on.' تارک here means the brow/forehead or crown (classical usage), and ما is the possessive 'our.' نبشت is the verbal root meaning 'inscribed/wrote' (past participial/historical sense used idiomatically as 'be inscribed'). Together: 'it must be inscribed upon our brow.'
segments.s659-g357: دل شاه 'the king's heart' (possessive relationship, noun before possessor is common in older Persian), نوشین‌روان is an epithet 'sweet‑souled' describing the king/heart, and شاد باد uses باد as an optative particle 'may (it) be happy.' The phrase is a wish/blessing for the king's heart.
segments.s659-g358: همیشه is the adverb 'always.' ز introduces the source 'from' (free from), followed by the coordinated nouns درد و غم 'pain and grief.' آزاد is the predicate adjective 'free,' and باد again is the optative 'may (it be).' The whole segment continues the blessing begun in the previous segment.
segments.s659-g359: اگر چند is a concessive phrase 'even if / though (even) somewhat'; چند here functions adverbially 'somewhat' or 'in some degree.' باشد is the classical subjunctive/present form 'be' (classical usage), and سرافراز is an adjective 'exalted/proud' modifying شاه 'the king.' The clause concedes the king's exalted status.
segments.s659-g360: به دستور is 'by/according to order/decree' (به + دستور). گردد is the classical third‑person subjunctive/passive-like 'becomes/is brought about.' دلارای is an epithet 'the beloved' (an honorific), and گاه 'time/occasion'; together دلارای گاه functions as 'the beloved's season/occasion' or 'the time of the favored one' which is said to 'come about' by decree.
segments.s659-g377: ز کار introduces a cause or reason ('because of the matter/affair'); آگهان here is an adverbial/parenthetical 'suddenly/unexpectedly' placed before the subject موبدی (the mobad) — a poetic inversion common in Classical Persian; نیکخواه is an adjective after the noun qualifying the mobad ('the benevolent/well‑wishing mobad').
segments.s659-g378: چنان بد که introduces a result clause: 'so (چنان) bad/upset (بد) that (که) ...'; برخاست is a past/stative verb 'rose/stood up' (here reported action), and بر پیش گاه is a prepositional phrase 'into the presence/of the court' with بر + پیش (before/in the presence of) + گاه (occasion/place).
segments.s659-g379: که introduces an explanatory/subordinate clause ('for/that'); گاهی is the adverb 'sometimes'; گنه is the object 'sin'; بگذرانی is the Classical Persian present/subjunctive form with the old prefix ب- (expressing 'you may let/you let pass' or a general/habitual action), and همی is the archaic/progressive/emphatic particle marking continuous or customary action.
segments.s659-g380: به بد نام is a circumstantial phrase 'with a bad name' = 'call by a bad reputation'; آنکس is the demonstrative 'that person'; نخوانی is the negative present/subjunctive/imperative form (ن‑ + خوانی) meaning 'do not call' (classical usage where خوانی = 'you call'); همی again adds emphatic/continuous sense ('do not habitually/therefore call').
segments.s659-g381: هم این را foregrounds the matter ('this, too') with را as the definite object/topic marker; دگر باره is a compound meaning 'again / another time / an added instance'; آویزش is a substantive 'attachment/pendant' used here metaphorically as 'an additional burden or consequence'; است is the copula 'is'.
segments.s659-g382: گنهکار is the noun 'sinner'; اگر چند is a concessive idiom ('if/though albeit' — 'even if' or 'although a little'), where اگر = 'if' and چند = 'some/though'; با پوزش است literally 'is with apology', i.e. 'is pardoned/has an excuse' — با + noun expresses accompaniment or condition and است is the copula.
segments.s659-g383: به پاسخ چنین بود is a formulaic opening: به پاسخ ('in reply'), چنین ('thus/so'), بود is the past tense 'was'; توقیع شاه is the nominal phrase 'the king's signed decree/edict' (توقیع = signed order, شاه = king). The whole segment states 'the king's decree was thus in reply.'
segments.s659-g384: که introduces the content of the decree ('that'); آنکس is 'that person/whoever'; the second که introduces the relative clause; خستو is an adjective 'weary/tormented' used here in the sense 'penitent/remorseful'; شود is the present/subjunctive of شدن 'become' (here 'becomes/turns'); بر + گناه marks the object/relation 'over/for sin' — together: 'that whoever becomes penitent for sin...'.
segments.s659-g369: چنین بود is a fixed poetic phrase meaning “thus it was” (چنین + past copula بود). تا گاه is a prepositional/time phrase: تا (until) + گاه (time/occasion), and نوشین‌روان is a descriptive name/appositive meaning “the sweet‑souled one” that specifies that time.
segments.s659-g370: The clause uses repeated همو (emphatic ‘the same one / himself’) with the past copula بود to enumerate roles: ‘همو بود شاه’ = ‘he himself was king’. The coordinating و links the two predicates, and the second همو repeats the emphatic subject before پهلوان (champion).
segments.s659-g371: Again همو + بود gives an emphatic subject: ‘he himself was’. جنگی here is used as a noun/adjective meaning ‘warrior’/‘warlike’ and موبد (mōbed) names the priestly office; the conjunction و links the two roles.
segments.s659-g372: This segment repeats the pattern but with different titles: سپهبد (spahbed) appears first, then the emphatic pronoun همو, the past copula بود, and a final coordinated predicate ‘و بخرد همو’ where بخرد is an adjective/noun ‘wise’ and the trailing همو again stresses it is the same person.
segments.s659-g373: به هرجای (to/at every place) + کارآگهان داشتی: کارآگهان (pl. of كارآگه) means people who know or investigate affairs (informers/agents) and داشتی is past second‑person ‘you had’. The unit says ‘you had (agents) in every place of business.’
segments.s659-g374: جهان را is the definite object ‘the world’. به دستور is an adverbial phrase lit. ‘by/with order’ (به + دستور), and نگذاشتی is the negative past ‘you did not let/leave’. Together it means ‘you did not leave the world without order’ / ‘you did not fail to keep the world ordered.’
segments.s659-g375: ز بسیار و اندک is a poetic pairing meaning ‘of great and small’ (from large and small), with ز (from/of) marking the genitive; ز کار جهان ties the phrase to ‘the affairs of the world’ (کار here = affairs/deeds).
segments.s659-g376: This clause states the negative result: ‘بد و نیک’ = ‘the bad and the good’; زو (from it/of it) + کس (anyone) + نکردی (you did not make/do) + نهان (hidden). Grammatically, نکردی نهان = ‘you did not make hidden’ (i.e. you did not hide), so the whole means you hid nothing — good or bad — from anyone.
segments.s659-g617: دگر گفت is a short clause meaning 'another (person) said' (دگر = 'another'); گفت is a past tense verb 'said'. کای is the vocative particle 'O' addressing someone, and نامور شهریار is a noun phrase 'renowned sovereign/king' (adjective before noun). The whole segment functions as the speaker’s opening: 'Another said, “O renowned king…”.'
segments.s659-g618: ز گنج تو افزون ز سیصد هزار is a verbless predicative clause: ز here = 'from/among' (poetic) and introduces the source/measure; گنج تو = 'your treasure/treasury'; افزون = 'more/greater' (adjective/degree); ز سیصد هزار = 'than three hundred thousand' (the second ز is the same poetic preposition used in comparative sense). Together: 'your treasure is greater than three hundred thousand.'
segments.s659-g619: درم داده‌ای is a second-person perfect construction 'you have given a dirham' (درم = dirham/small coin; داده‌ای = 'you have given'). مرد درویش را is the definite/specific object marked by را ('the poor/dervish man' as the recipient). Note the verb precedes the object here, and را marks that object as definite/specific.
segments.s659-g620: بسی پروریده تن خویش را is a clause describing the subject's self-regard: بسی is an intensifier meaning 'much/very' (adverbial), پروریده is a past participle 'nurtured/pampered' used predicatively, and تن خویش را = 'his/your own body' (تن = body; خوی + ش form خویش 'one's own', with را marking it as object). Word order places the participle before the object it governs.
segments.s659-g621: چنین گفت = 'thus said' (چنین = thus/so; گفت = said). کاین هم = 'this also' or 'that likewise' (کاین = this/that, هم = also). به فرمان ماست = 'is by our command' (به introduces the instrument/measure 'by', فرمان = decree/command, ما = our, ست = copula 'is'). The whole clause reports the speaker's decree: 'Thus he said that this too is by our order.'
segments.s659-g622: به ارزانیان چیز‌بخشی رواست is a general statement of propriety: به = 'for' or 'to' (marks the beneficiary class), ارزانیان = 'the givers/bestowers' (those who give), چیز‌بخشی = 'giving/distribution of things' (a verbal-noun), رواست = 'is fitting/permissible' (روا = fitting/permissible; ست = copula). So: 'It is fitting for givers to distribute things.'
segments.s659-g623: دگر گفت = 'another said' as before. کای شاه is the vocative 'O king'. نادیده رنج literally pairs the adjective نادیده 'unseen/unmindful' with رنج 'hardship/suffering' to mean 'heedless/unaware of hardship' or 'without regard to hardship'—a poetic condensed phrase where the adjective precedes the noun it modifies.
segments.s659-g624: ز بخشش فراوان تهی ماند گنج is the result clause: ز = 'from/because of' (introducing cause), بخشش = 'giving/donation' (the act of bestowing), فراوان = 'abundant/excessive', تهی ماند = 'became empty/was left empty' (تهی = empty; ماند = remained/was left), گنج = 'treasure/treasury' (subject at the end). Word order places the affected noun گنج after the verb phrase, a poetic inversion meaning 'the treasury remained empty because of abundant giving.'
segments.s659-g161: شنیده is a past participle used adverbially ("having heard"); بگفتند is the past-tense plural verb "they said/spoke." با شهریار follows and marks the addressee ("with/to the king"); the participle before the verb is a common classical/Persian compressed clause meaning "having heard, they said to the king."
segments.s659-g162: دلش = دل + enclitic pronoun ش ("his heart"). گشت is the intransitive past of 'گشتن' used as 'became/turned.' زان ("from that/thereon") governs the source-cause; پاسخ او = "that answer of his." فگار is used adjectivally ("piercing, stabbing") so the clause literally reads "his heart became (as) piercing from that answer," i.e. "his heart was pierced by that reply."
segments.s659-g163: به + ایوانش + بردند = a verb phrase 'they carried/led (بردند) into/to his iwan (ایوانش).' زان is the demonstrative 'that' and premodifies تنگ جای ('narrow place'); word order (verb before the modifying phrase) is typical of the epic/poetic compression.
segments.s659-g164: به دستوری here means 'by/according to a command' (به used in the instrumental/causal sense). پاکدل رهنمای is a noun phrase 'the pure‑hearted guide/adviser' (adjective before noun, no ezāfe shown in the verse); taken together the phrase reads 'by the command of the pure‑hearted guide.'
segments.s659-g165: برین نیز = بر + این (here spelled ین) + نیز → 'on this too/with regard to this as well.' بگذشت is the impersonal/past verb 'passed' (of time). چندی normally means 'for a while' here (temporal adverb) and سپهر ('heavens/sky') is the poetic noun placed after the temporal phrase — overall: 'some time passed on this as well.'
segments.s659-g166: پر is the prepositional/adjectival 'full (of)' ("full of X"); آژنگ is an archaic/rare lexical item and here is the noun that fills 'پر' (the exact nuance is archaic/contextual); شد is 'became,' and روی بوزرجمهر = 'the face of Bozorgmehr.' The construction means 'Bozorgmehr's face became full of ājang.'
segments.s659-g167: دلش = 'his heart.' تنگتر is the comparative/adverbial form 'more tight/narrower'; گشت is 'became.' و simply links the two predicates, and باریک شد is another resulting predicate 'became thin/slender.' Together they describe increasing constriction of feeling: 'his heart grew more constricted and became thin.'
segments.s659-g168: دوچمش is a fused form meaning 'his two eyes' (دو + چشم + enclitic ش); ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of' marking cause; اندیشه 'thought' is the cause, and تاریک شد = 'became dark.' The clause literally: 'his two eyes, from thought, became dark' → 'his eyes darkened with thought.'
segments.s659-g513: دگر گفت is an introductory phrase meaning “another (one) said” (دگر = “another”) and is followed by a vocative: کای is the archaic/vocative address “O Kay” (a royal title). با خرد گشته جفت is a small adjectival/participial phrase modifying the addressee: با خرد (“with wisdom”) + گشته (perfect/participial sense “become/turned” = “who has become/been”) + جفت (“matched/paired”), so the whole chunk reads “O Kay, who has become paired with wisdom.”
segments.s659-g514: به میدان خراسان سالار گفت is a prepositional phrase plus a reporting verb: به میدان خراسان = “to/at the field of Khorasan” (به + noun phrase), سالار = “the commander/chief,” and گفت is the speech verb “said.” The phrase identifies the addressee and setting (he spoke to the Khorasan commander in the field).
segments.s659-g515: که introduces a reported/relative clause (“that”); گرزاسب را is the personal name with the object marker را (marking Garzasp as the patient). بازکرد is a past action verb meaning here “released/relieved/dismissed” (literal senses listed are somewhat different; context gives the sense “removed from duty”), and او ز کار = “him from work/office” (او = he; ز is the poetic form of از = “from”; کار = work/office). The clause means “that (he) relieved Garzasp from his office.”
segments.s659-g516: چه گفت is a question phrase “what said / what did (he) say”; اندرین کار = “in this matter / about this affair” (اندرین = in/among these; کار = affair); او شهریار places the agent/target — here “the king” (او = he; شهریار = king). The whole segment asks, “What did the king say about this affair?”
segments.s659-g517: چنین داد پاسخ is a formulaic reporting clause: چنین = “thus/so,” داد here is the verb “gave” (داد پاسخ = “gave a reply”), and پاسخ = “reply/answer.” که then introduces the content of that reply: فرمان ما = “our command/order” (فرمان = command; ما = our).
segments.s659-g518: نورزید is a verb built on ورزیدن (to practice/observe); here with the negative/defective nuance it means “did not heed/did not observe” our command (contextually negative is implied). و = “and”; بنهفت (a problematic/obscure form in the lexical list) is best read here as part of the predicate meaning that the covenant was violated; پیمان ما = “our covenant” (پیمان = covenant; ما = our). Together: “he did not heed our command and he broke our covenant.”
segments.s659-g519: بفرمودمش is a compact past-tense causative with enclitic object: بفرمود = “(he) commanded/ordered” (formal causative stem) + -مَش/-مش = “him” (object clitic), so “I/he ordered him.” تا introduces purpose/result “so that / in order that”; به = “to/for”; ارزانیان is a plural noun (contextual group, glosses vary) — here the indirect beneficiaries/recipients. The clause means “I ordered him so that he would (do something) for the arzaniyan.”
segments.s659-g520: گشاید is a present/subjunctive/optative form of گشودن “to open” (here: “to open / should open”); در گنج = “in/into the treasury” (در = in; گنج = treasure/treasury); سود و زیان = “profit and loss” (a fixed pair meaning benefit and detriment). The whole segment expresses the intended action: “to open in the treasury (so as to handle) profit and loss.”
segments.s659-g273: بفرمود is the honorific past of فرمودن 'to say/command' (he ordered/said). تا introduces a subordinate/purpose clause here ('that/so that'), followed by the past verb رفت 'he went' (simple past). نزدیک تخت is a locative phrase 'near the throne' (نزدیک + noun), placed after the verb as the goal/place of the action.
segments.s659-g274: دل شاه کسری is a possessive noun group 'the heart of King Khosrow' (possessed + possessor). غمی گشت is a predicative construction where غمی (a state of grief) combines with گشت (past of گشتن/شدن 'to become') to mean 'became sorrowful'; سخت is an adverbial intensifier here ('deeply/very').
segments.s659-g275: که here is causal 'because/for'. داننده را uses the object/topic marker را to mark 'the knower' as the locus being evaluated; چشم بینا ('a discerning/clear-seeing eye') is the noun phrase that follows and is negated by ندید (past negative of دیدن 'did not see'). Literally: 'because he did not see in the knower a discerning eye.'
segments.s659-g276: This is a poetic/fixed idiom: بسی (intensifier) + باد سرد ('a cold wind') + از جگر ('from the liver', figurative 'from the heart/seat of feeling') + بر کشید (past of برکشیدن 'to draw up/out'). Literally 'a very cold wind he drew from his liver' i.e. 'a cold shiver ran through him.' Some provided lemma senses do not include the exact poetic meanings, so the closest sense indices are given where available.
segments.s659-g277: همی‌کرد is the imperfective/progressive past 'was doing/kept doing'. پوزش is the noun 'apology/excuse' as the object; ازان کار شاه is a genitive phrase 'for that deed of the king' (ازان = 'from/for that', کار = 'deed', شاه = 'the king'). The whole segment means 'he kept offering excuses for that act of the king.'
segments.s659-g278: کزو is a relative/adverbial 'from which / by which' referring back to that act; داشت آزار uses داشت plus noun آزار to indicate that harm existed/was caused (i.e. 'caused harm' or 'there was harm'); بر is the prepositional particle 'upon/against', and بی‌گناه is 'the innocent.' Together: 'by which he had caused harm to the innocent.'
segments.s659-g279: پس is 'then/afterwards'. از here means 'about/concerning' (linking the following nouns as topics), so از روم و قیصر = 'about Rome and the Caesar'. زبان برگشاد is an idiom 'opened his tongue' (زبان 'tongue/speech' + برگشاد 'opened') meaning 'he began to speak.'
segments.s659-g280: همی‌کرد again marks a continuous/persistent action 'kept doing'. زان is a demonstrative 'from that/thereof' pointing back; قفل 'lock' and درج 'inscription/entry' are the objects, and یاد here is a verbal noun meaning 'mention/recall' — so 'he kept mentioning that lock and that inscription.'
segments.s659-g449: چنین داد پاسخ که is a fixed poetic phrase meaning 'he answered thus' (literally 'thus gave answer that'). داد is a past-tense verb 'gave' placed before its noun پاسخ in an inverted, literary order. The clause دندان نبود that follows is a simple past negative: دندان (subject) + نبود (past negative of 'to be') = 'there were no teeth.'
segments.s659-g450: مکیدن جز از شیر پستان نبود is a verbless nominal statement with نبود finishing the clause: مکیدن ('sucking' as a noun/masdar) is the topic; جز از شیر پستان ('except from the breast's milk') restricts it; نبود (= 'was not' / 'there was not') negates existence — i.e. 'there was nothing to suck except the breast's milk.' Word order places the gerundic noun first and the negator at the end, a common poetic pattern.
segments.s659-g451: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'; چو دندان برآمد = 'when the teeth emerged' (دندان subject, برآمد past 'came up/appeared'). ببالید پشت is the main clause with poetic inversion: verb ببالید 'grew proud/was inflated' followed by its subject پشت 'the back'. So the whole means 'when the teeth appeared, the back became proud.'
segments.s659-g452: همی (a continuous/habitual marker) + گوشت جویم (object 'meat/flesh' before the verb, then جویم 'I seek') yields 'I kept seeking flesh.' The trailing temporal clause چو گشتم درشت ('when I became large/coarse') uses گشتم (past 'I became') and the adjective درشت; poetically the temporal clause is placed after the main clause.
segments.s659-g453: یکی گفت is 'someone said.' گیرم here is a concessive/hypothetical present-subjunctive form meaning 'suppose I take' or 'let's say I were to take'; کنون = 'now'; مهتری is the noun 'preeminence' or 'being chief' as the complement of گیرم. The short clause therefore means 'Someone said, "Suppose now I become preeminent."'
segments.s659-g454: به‌رای و به‌دانش are two prepositional/relational phrases 'in counsel' and 'in knowledge' (literally 'with regard to opinion' and 'with regard to learning') qualifying the domain of superiority. ز ما ('than us') marks the standard of comparison and مهتری repeats the nominal 'preeminence.' The whole string attaches to the previous clause: 'preeminence in counsel and in knowledge over us.' Word order is poetic; the comparison marker ز + pronoun follows the qualifiers.
segments.s659-g455: چرا is the interrogative 'why.' برگذشتی is the 2nd-person singular past tense 'you passed by/overlooked' (بر- prefix + گذشتن). ز شاهنشهان uses the poetic genitive/ablative ز 'from/of' with شاهنشهان (pl. 'kings of kings' = emperors) as the object of the verbal phrase: 'Why did you pass by the emperors?'
segments.s659-g456: دو دیده (two eyes) is the grammatical subject. به‌رای تو here is a prepositional/relational phrase roughly 'for you' / 'in your keeping' (poetic usage), and دارد جهان places the verb دارد ('has') before the noun جهان ('the world') by inversion: literally 'two eyes for you the world has' = 'the world has two eyes for you' — i.e. the world watches or regards you attentively.
segments.s659-g601: چنین داد پاسخ is a formulaic phrase ‘thus gave reply’ where چنین (thus) fronts the clause and داد (gave) is the verbal element taking پاسخ (reply) as its object; که introduces the content clause that follows. اندر جهان is a locative prepositional phrase (‘in the world’) placed at the end of this segment to set the scene for the following optative.
segments.s659-g602: به ما is an indirect-object/benefactive phrase ‘for us’; شاد is the predicate adjective ‘happy’; بادا is an archaic optative/volitive particle meaning ‘may it be’ (wishing/blessing). کهان و مهان is the nominative/subject group the optative refers to — here read as ‘the ancients/elders (کَهَان) and the guests/visitors (مهان)’ — placed after the verb phrase as is common in classical poetic order.
segments.s659-g603: دگر گفت functions as a short narrative clause ‘another/one more said’ (دگر = another). کای is the archaic vocative particle ‘O’ addressing شاه; برترمنش is a postpositive epithet ‘of haughty bearing’ describing the king. The sequence is the classical poetic order for an addressed phrase.
segments.s659-g604: همی is an iterative/adverbial particle meaning ‘continually/always’ and, with کنند/کند constructions, marks habitual action. زشتگویت is a deverbal/nominal compound ‘ugly/abusive speech’ modifying the reproach; کند is the present/habitual-form verb ‘does/makes’ and سرزنش is the noun ‘reproach’ that serves as the act being done. The segment therefore reads literally as ‘continually (with) abusive-speech does reproach.’
segments.s659-g605: که introduces reported content/explanation ‘that / for that reason’. چندین گزافه is a quantified noun phrase ‘many/various vain/exaggerated things/boasts’; ببخشید is a past/perfective giving verb (classical poetic form of بخشیدن here) meaning ‘bestowed/gave’. گنج is the direct object ‘treasure’ and in context indicates what the boasting is tied to (or what should be earned).
segments.s659-g606: ز + گرد آوردن forms a source/ablative phrase ‘from/the toil of gathering’ where گرد آوریدن (compound of گرد ‘about/around’ used here in the verbal sense ‘to go about/collect’ + آوردن ‘to bring’) means ‘to collect/amass’. ندیده‌ست is a perfective negative ‘has not seen’ (ن- + دیده‌ست), and رنج is the noun ‘toil/suffering’ — together: ‘has not seen the toil of gathering.’
segments.s659-g607: Again, چنین داد پاسخ is the fixed formula ‘thus gave answer’. که introduces the reported clause; آن خواسته is a demonstrative noun phrase ‘that desire/wish’ serving as the topic of the reported answer.
segments.s659-g608: کزو is a relative/adverbial pronoun ‘from which / by which’; گنج ما is the noun phrase ‘our treasure’; باشد here is a present/subjunctive copula (classical use, here plausibly ‘may be / is’) and آراسته is a predicative adjective ‘adorned/ornamented’. The clause therefore reads ‘from which our treasure is/may be adorned’ — i.e. ‘the desire by which our treasure shall be made splendid.’
segments.s659-g201: چو is a subordinating particle meaning 'when' here. گشتند is a past tense verb ('became') placed before its subject in the poetic V–S word order; although انجمن is grammatically singular, the plural verb form reflects a collective sense common in epic diction. یک انجمن ناتوان = 'an assembly (that was) powerless', with ناتوان as the predicate adjective describing انجمن.
segments.s659-g202: غمی is the noun 'a grief' (subject) and شد is the past of شدن 'became'. دل شاه نوشین‌روان is a genitive phrase 'the heart of the sweet‑souled king' (شاه = king, نوشین‌روان an epithet). The clause literally reads 'a grief became in the heart of the sweet‑souled king' = 'grief filled the king's heart.'
segments.s659-g203: همی‌گفت is an iterative/past continuous formula meaning 'he kept saying' or 'he was saying.' کاین is the demonstrative 'this/that' introducing the reported content, and راز گردان سپهر = 'the secret of the turning/revolving heavens' where گردان is an adjectival/participial notion 'revolving' modifying سپهر (the heavens). The whole segment is the content clause headed by همی‌گفت.
segments.s659-g204: بیارد here functions as an optative/jussive form 'may it be brought' or 'bring (so that it be brought)' — a classical subjunctive/imperative flavor used after a verb of saying. باندیشه = 'into/unto the thought' (بـ + اندیشه), and بوزرجمهر names Bozorgmehr (dative/goal of the bringing). Note: the provided lemma/senses for بیارد do not match the verbal use (it appears here as a verb 'bring'); therefore the sense index is set to -1.
segments.s659-g205: شد 'became' introduces a change of state. از درد marks the cause ('from/because of pain'). دانا is an epithet 'the wise (one)' identifying the subject. دلش = 'his heart' (دل + enclitic ش = his) and پر ز درد = 'full of pain/sorrow' (پر = 'full (of)', ز = poetic 'of/from', درد = sorrow). The clause strings cause, subject epithet, and resultant condition.
segments.s659-g206: This compressed poetic clause describes further outward signs. The initial برو is a poetic contraction (functionally 'بر او' = 'upon him' / 'on his (brow)') used to locate the action on the person; پر ز چین کرد = 'made (it) full of folds/wrinkles' where کرد is the verb 'made/caused' and پر ز چین = 'full of wrinkles.' و links the next result: رخساره زرد = 'his visage/complexion became yellow/pale.' Note that some forms are archaic/elliptical typical of epic style.
segments.s659-g207: شهنشاه is the subject 'the emperor.' چون دید is a temporal/causal clause 'when' or 'on seeing' (چون = 'when/since', دید = 'he saw'). ز اندیشه رنج uses ز to mark cause/source ('from/through thought') and رنج = 'suffering/anguish' — together 'the anguish caused by thinking.' The sequence gives the reason for the emperor's following action.
segments.s659-g208: بفرمود is the courtly/honorific past 'he ordered/commanded/said (to do so).' تا introduces purpose ('so that/in order that') and governs the following infinitival/imperative idea. جامه دستی = 'a garment at hand' (جامه = robe, دستی = 'at hand/readily available'). ز گنج = 'from the treasure' (ز = 'from', گنج = 'treasure'). The implied verb after تا is 'bring/fetch'; the sentence therefore means he ordered that a ready robe be brought from the treasury.
segments.s659-g153: چه ... چه is a correlative construction meaning 'whether ... or ...'. با introduces an instrumental accompaniment ('with/by'). گنج و تختی lists two nouns joined by و ('and'); تختی is an archaic/poetic form for 'throne' (noun + ـی). رنج سخت is noun + adjective (noun first, modifier after) meaning 'severe hardship'.
segments.s659-g154: ببندیم is 1st-plural subjunctive/jussive (بـ + root بند + ـیم) here meaning 'let us bind/pack (our things)'. هر دو = 'both (of them)'; هر functions as the distributive/quantifier before دو. بناکام (بناکام) is an adjective 'unsuccessful/frustrated' modifying رخت. رخت here is used in the idiom رخت بستن / رخت بستن meaning 'to pack up / departure', so it refers to baggage/departure.
segments.s659-g155: نه ... نه is the paired negation 'neither ... nor ...'. این (this) is the subject; پای دارد literally 'has a foot' = 'has standing/foothold' with دارد as the present indicative 'has'. بگیتی (ب + گیتی) is a poetic locative/adverbial 'in the world'. آن = 'that' (the counterpart in the correlative).
segments.s659-g156: سرآید is a present/future-like verb 'comes/occurs' (here general/gnomic sense 'will come' or 'come to pass'). همی is an emphatic/adverbial particle meaning 'always/continually' or 'indeed' in classical usage. نیک و بد is the coordinated pair 'good and bad'. بی‌گمان is an adverb 'undoubtedly' qualifying the whole clause.
segments.s659-g157: ز is a poetic preposition meaning 'from' or 'because of' and here introduces the circumstance. سختی گذر کردن is a deverbal/infinitival expression: گذر (passage) + کردن (light verb) = 'to pass' (to pass through hardship). آسان بود is past copula 'was easy' (بود marking past/state).
segments.s659-g158: دل is the subject 'heart' (metonymically 'minds/spirits'). تاجداران is plural 'the crowned ones / sovereigns' (those who wear the crown). هراسان is an adjective 'frightened/terrified' predicated of دل, and بود is the past copula 'was'.
segments.s659-g159: خردمند و دژخیم is a coordinated subject 'the wise (one) and the dēžkhīm (lit. 'fortress-cruel', here 'executioner/oppressor, cruel one')'. باز is an adverb 'again/returned', and آمدند is the 3rd-person plural past 'they came/returned', agreeing with the compound subject.
segments.s659-g160: بر شاه is a prepositional phrase 'to/toward the king' (بر used as 'to/toward' in classical register). گردن‌فراز is a predicative/adverbial adjective 'neck-raised' = 'proud, with head held high' describing the manner of آمدند, and آمدند is the plural past 'they came'.
segments.s659-g313: فرستیم is the 1st-person-plural past tense of 'to send' (we sent / we send, narrative). زین پس is a temporal phrase 'from now on / henceforth' (زین here is archaic/poetic in sense 'this/now' and follows with پس). به تو is the dative 'to you'. باژ و ساو are two coordinated nouns (names or terms) joined by و 'and' and function as the direct objects of فرستیم.
segments.s659-g314: که introduces a clause of explanation or reason ('for / because / that'). این مرز دارند is a verbless/compound idea where دارند (third-person plural present) means 'they have / (this) possesses' and مرز is the subject or thing possessed ('this border'). با + باژ + تاو is an instrumental/associative phrase 'with Bāzh Tāw' (با links the possession to what it is associated with); تاو here is uncertain/possibly a name or poetic noun.
segments.s659-g315: وگر is a conditional conjunction 'and if / but if'. باز here means 'again' or 'once more' (adverb). مانند ازین مایه چیز is a noun phrase 'anything like/from this kind of stuff' — مانند 'like', ازین مایه 'of this material/kind' and چیز 'thing' is the head noun.
segments.s659-g316: نخواهند is the 3rd-person plural present/future of خواه (here negative 'they will not want / they do not wish'); ازین مرزها 'from these borders' (ازین = 'from these', مرزها plural 'borders'). باژ نیز stands after as the thing they would not want 'the baž as well' (نیز = 'also / even'), so the clause states they will not desire even the baž from those borders.
segments.s659-g317: چو is a subordinating conjunction meaning 'when' here. دانا is the subject 'the wise (man)'. ز + گوینده marks the source 'from the speaker'. پاسخ شنید is a simple past clause 'he heard the answer' (شنید is 3rd-person-singular past of 'to hear'). The whole segment gives the temporal clause 'when the wise man heard the speaker's answer'.
segments.s659-g318: زبان برگشاد is literal 'his tongue opened' (زبان = speech/utterance; برگشاد is a past participle/adjective 'opened'). آفرین گسترید is a verbal phrase where آفرین functions as 'praise' or 'acclamation' and گسترید (3rd-person singular past of گستراندن) means 'he spread / he uttered widely' — together 'he opened his tongue and let loose praise' (i.e., he praised aloud).
segments.s659-g319: که here introduces a wish/benediction clause ('that / so that / may it be that'). همواره 'always'. شاه جهان 'king of the world' (compound noun). شاد باد is the classical optative construction: شاد (happy) + باد (optative particle 'may it be'), giving 'may he be happy'.
segments.s659-g320: This continues the wish: سخن‌دان 'eloquent / man of speech' as a quality wished for; و با بخت و با داد 'and with fortune and with justice' — با introduces accompanying qualities (with fortune, with justice). The final باد repeats the optative 'may it be', so the whole segment lists wished qualities for the king.
segments.s659-g225: This clause contains a reported question introduced by که (a relative / subordinating particle): 'که' here links the question to reported speech ('that' / 'which'). The phrase 'این درج را چی ست اندر نهان' literally orders as 'this inscription (marked by را as definite object) what is in/within hidden' — چی ست is the interrogative 'what is', اندر = 'in/within', نهان = 'hidden/secret'. Note the poetic word order (object/phrase before the copula).
segments.s659-g226: بگویند is the reportative verb ('they say' / 'it is said') introducing what follows; in classical/poetic Persian بگویند can appear where modern Persian uses گفتند. فرزانگان جهان is the agent phrase 'the sages of the world' (فرزانگان = sages/wise men, جهان = world). The verb is used to attribute the question to these sages.
segments.s659-g227: به دل گفتم is an idiom 'I said to my heart' = 'I thought' (گفتم is first-person past 'I said'); the noun phrase 'این راز پوشیده چهر' = 'this secret/hidden face' where راز پوشیده ('hidden secret') modifies چهر ('face/appearance'). Word order is poetic: demonstrative + noun + adjective + head noun.
segments.s659-g228: ببیند is a verbal form 'may/let (someone) see' or simple present/subjunctive 'sees'; مگر here functions as 'except/only' or 'perhaps/if only' in poetic usage implying exclusivity or hope; جان بوزرجمهر = 'the soul of Bozorgmehr' (جان = soul/life, proper name follows). Together: 'perhaps only Bozorgmehr's soul will see (it).'
segments.s659-g229: چوبشنید is a fused poetic form ('چو' + 'بشنید') meaning 'when he heard' or 'on hearing'; it introduces the temporal clause. The proper name بوزرجمهر is the subject and این سخن ('these / this words') is the object 'this speech/remark'.
segments.s659-g230: دلش = دل + -ش (his heart). پرشد is past tense 'became full / was filled'. از introduces cause/source 'from' or 'with', and رنج و درد کهن is a coordinated phrase 'ancient/longstanding suffering and pain' where کهن modifies the noun pair. Overall: 'his heart filled from (with) ancient suffering and pain.'
segments.s659-g231: ز زندان بیامد = 'came from prison' (ز = poetic 'from', بیامد = came). سرو here is used metaphorically ('the cypress' = a tall/beautiful man) as the subject; تن بشست is a second clause 'he washed (his) body' (تن = body, بشست = washed). The two verbs describe consecutive actions.
segments.s659-g232: به پیش جهانداور آمد نخست = 'he came before the world-judge first.' به پیش = 'to the presence of', جهانداور = 'world-judge' (an epithet for the king or arbiter), آمد = 'came', and نخست = 'first' (adverb) — poetic word order places the adverb after the verb.
segments.s659-g593: چنین داد پاسخ is a classical word order where چنان(ین) 'thus/so' modifies داد 'gave' and پاسخ 'answer' is the noun: literally 'thus gave (an) answer'. The following که introduces the reported content (a quoted clause). یزدان سپاس is a verbless exclamation 'God — praise/thanks', functioning as the content of that answer.
segments.s659-g594: که here continues the quoted clause 'that ...'. از ما کسی نیست اندر هراس is an existential negative: از ما 'from/among us', کسی نیست 'there is no one' (کسی takes the negative predicate نیست), اندر هراس 'in fear' (اندر = in/within). The whole means 'there is no one among us who is in fear.'
segments.s659-g595: This phrase uses inverted, archaic word order: فزون 'more / increased' + کرد 'make/do' + باید 'it is necessary / one must' + بدیشان 'to them' + نگاه 'attention/regard'. Together it means 'one must increase (the) attention toward them.' Note that کرد is the main verb 'to make/increase' and باید expresses necessity.
segments.s659-g596: اگر ... و‌گر sets up a two-way conditional/contrast: اگر با گناهند 'if they are with sin' (i.e. guilty) و‌گر بی‌گناه 'or if innocent'. با marks the condition 'with (sin)', and گناهند is the present plural copula 'they are guilty'; بی‌گناه is adjectival 'innocent' with the copula understood.
segments.s659-g597: دگر here means 'again' (an adverb). گفت is the past-tense reporting verb 'said' introducing direct speech (note: the provided sense list lacked the verbal sense, so the sense index is unknown). کای شاه is a vocative 'O king' (کای = O). با فر و هوش is an ablative/causal phrase 'by/with (your) glory and wisdom' modifying the effect described in the next segment.
segments.s659-g598: جهان شد 'the world became' uses شد (past of شدن) to mark change of state. پر + ز/of (here expressed as پر آواز ...) means 'full (of)'; آواز خنیا و نوش is a nominal grouping 'the sound/song of singing and delight' — i.e. 'full of the sounds of singing and merriment.'
segments.s659-g599: This is a compound subject framed by a conditional particle: توانگر 'the rich/a rich man' (here plural/general) و‌گر 'or if' introduces the alternative مردم زیردست 'the people under (you)/subordinates'. The phrase lists social groups that will be the subject of the following clause.
segments.s659-g600: شب آید شود is a two-verb classical sequence: آید 'comes' + شود 'becomes' — 'when night comes it becomes'. پر ز + noun = 'full of (something)'; آوایِ مست 'the sound/voice of the intoxicated' (مست used adjectivally) yields 'full of the sounds of drunken singing.' ز is the poetic genitive 'of'.
segments.s659-g657: یکی گفت = 'someone said' (subject + past verb). کای is an archaic/vocative form 'O' used to address a king; شاه is the addressee. کهتر نواز is an epithet formed from کهتر 'the lesser/inferior' plus نواز (a participial/adjectival form meaning 'kind/tender toward'), describing the king as one who favors or treats kindly the lowly.
segments.s659-g658: چرا = 'why' (question word). گشتی is the perfect/past form of گشتن used here as 'you have become/you turned' (second-person reference in narrative speech). اکنون 'now' is a temporal adverb; چنین 'so/thus' is an intensifier; دیر 'late' is an adverb/adjective; یاز here is the archaic/poetic token meaning 'again' or 'anew' (so دیر یاز = 'late again' / 'become late again').
segments.s659-g659: چنین داد پاسخ = 'gave such an answer' (چنین 'thus' + داد 'gave' + پاسخ 'answer'); که introduces the reported clause ('that/that is'). با بخردان = 'with the wise' (با = with; بخردان = plural/oblique of بخرد, 'the wise/prudent').
segments.s659-g660: همانم همان = emphatic repetition meaning 'I am the same' (همانم = 'I am the same', the following همان reinforces it). نیز = 'also/likewise'. با موبدان = 'with the mobeds/priests' (با = with; موبدان = plural of موبد). The whole segment reiterates equality of treatment toward both groups.
segments.s659-g661: چو = 'when' (archaic/poetic conjunction). آواز آهرمن = 'the voice of Ahriman' (possessive/attributive sequence: آواز + name). آید is the present/simple narrative form of آمدن 'to come' (here 'reaches'). به گوش = 'to the ear' (prepositional phrase marking reception).
segments.s659-g662: نماند = 'does not remain/there is not left' (negative perfective/state verb used impersonally). به دل رای = 'in the heart (دل) [there is] counsel/opinion (رای)' — رای here means 'plan/counsel'. و = 'and'. با مغز هوش = literally 'with the brain, (there is) intelligence' (با + مغز marking the locus, هوش = intellect/awareness). Thus two loci (دل and مغز) lose counsel and intelligence when the devil's voice is heard.
segments.s659-g663: بپرسید is a past narrative form meaning 'asked' (third-person past). موبد is the subject 'the mobed/priest'. ز شاه زمین = 'from the king of the land' (ز = 'from' in poetic usage; شاه زمین = 'king of the land' as a compound title). Note the verb-subject order typical of narrative Persian poetry.
segments.s659-g664: سخن راند = literally 'drove/spoke forth a word' (سخن = speech/word; راند is the past of راندن 'to drive' used here metaphorically as 'uttered/expressed'). از = 'about/concerning' (here marking the topic). پادشاهی و دین = 'kingship and religion' (topics of his speech).
segments.s659-g257: همانگه is a temporal adverb placed at the beginning ('at that moment'). زنی دیگر is a noun phrase in postnominal order ('a woman, another'), with دیگر after the noun meaning 'another'. آمد پدید is a two‑word verbal expression where آمد (past third person singular 'came') precedes the stative/verb پدید ('appeared'); together they mean 'appeared'.
segments.s659-g258: بپرسید is a past‑tense verb (3rd person singular) 'she asked' with the b‑ prefix common in older/poetic Persian. چو here is temporal 'when'. ترجمانش is the noun 'his/her interpreter' with the enclitic -ش marking possession. بدید is the past tense verb 'saw' (object before verb: ترجمانش بدید = 'saw his interpreter').
segments.s659-g259: که‌ای here functions as a vocative/interrogative 'O / which', addressing the woman (که‌ای زن = 'O woman' / 'what woman'). تو را is the dative/possessive construction 'to you' (used to indicate possession here). بچه و شوی are coordinated nouns 'child and شوی' followed by the copula هست at the end; هست is the copula 'is/are' forming the existential/possessive question 'do you have...?' Note: شوی in the provided senses appears as a verbal root and does not match the noun sense 'husband' (so sense index is not available for that word here).
segments.s659-g260: وگر is the conditional particle 'if' or 'but if'. یک تنی is an idiomatic numeral + تنی 'one person / a single body' (تنی here = 'a certain/single (person)'). باد is the archaic/poetic optative particle 'may/let' (used in older Persian), here contributing to a conditional sense before the verb. داری is the present second‑person singular 'you have/hold'. به‌دست is a locative/agentive phrase 'in (your) hand' or 'at hand' and follows the verb phrase.
segments.s659-g261: بدو is a prepositional/indirect object phrase 'to her/to him' (indicating the addressee of the speech). گفت here is the past tense 'said' (the provided senses list did not include the 'said' sense, so the sense index is not available). شوی‌ست is a fused/archaic copular form meaning roughly 'you are / you would be' (future/optative nuance); اگر introduces the condition 'if'; بچه نیست is 'there is no child' with نیست as the negated copula.
segments.s659-g262: چو is temporal 'when'. پاسخ is the noun 'reply/answer' and شنیدی is the past or perfective form 'you heard' (here used temporally: 'when you have heard the answer'). بر من is a prepositional phrase 'before/on me' (here 'before me' / 'in my presence'). مایست is an archaic/poetic verbal form (only the one obscure sense is provided); in context it functions as an imperative/command or stative meaning 'stand / be stationed' (i.e., 'stand before me').
segments.s659-g263: همانگه is the temporal adverb 'at that moment'. سه‌دیگر is a compound meaning 'the three together' (i.e., three women acting collectively). زن is the noun 'woman' and آمد پدید again is the verbal pair 'came/appeared' with آمد (past 'came') plus پدید ('appeared').
segments.s659-g264: بیامد is past 'they came'. بر او is the direction 'to him/toward him' (preposition + pronoun). بگفت is a past reporting verb 'they said/spoke' and شنید is 'they heard' or 'listened' (the two verbs joined by و 'and' describe their actions toward him). Word order is typical older Persian: object/dative (او) follows the preposition and precedes the verbal forms.
segments.s659-g233: همی‌بود is a habitual/continuative past copula meaning 'he remained/kept being.' ترسان is a predicative adjective 'afraid.' ز introduces the cause/source 'from / because of,' and آزار شاه is a genitive phrase 'the king's harm/oppression' that completes the reason why he remained afraid.
segments.s659-g234: This is a verbless, coordinated observation: جهاندار 'the sovereign' modified by the prepositive adjective phrase پر خشم 'full of anger' (پر = 'full (of)'). The conjunction و links the next brief clause او بیگناه, where او is the pronoun 'he' and بیگناه is the predicative adjective 'innocent' (the copula 'was' is omitted as is common in poetry).
segments.s659-g235: Two coordinated noun phrases describe the state: شب تیره 'the night [was] dark' and روز پیدا 'the day [was] visible/apparent.' The final نبـود is the past-negative copula 'was not,' applying to the second coordinated phrase (and by sense to the overall observation).
segments.s659-g236: بدان سان is a fixed adverbial compound meaning 'in that manner / so,' and که here introduces the result clause ('so that'). پیغام خسرو is 'the king's message.' شنود is a third-person verbal form 'hears' (under the scope of the prior negation it yields 'could not hear' or 'there was not a situation in which one heard').
segments.s659-g237: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when.' خورشید is the subject 'the sun.' بنمود is the past-tense verb 'showed / revealed.' تاج از فراز is a noun phrase 'the crown from the heights / on high' (از فراز = 'from/on high'). The clause sets the temporal scene for what follows.
segments.s659-g238: بپوشید is a past action 'it covered / put on' (classical poetic use with ب-). روی شب تیره = 'the face of the dark night' (روی = 'face' used with a genitival noun), and باز at the end means 'again.' The implied subject (from the previous clause) carries over, so the action is to be read with reference to the sun/night scene just introduced.
segments.s659-g239: باختر نگه کرد is a short verb phrase 'he cast/kept his gaze to the west' (باختر = 'west,' نگه = 'gaze/watch,' کرد = past 'did/made'). In classical Persian poetry the subject (here the name) commonly follows the verb, so بوزرجمهر is the actor 'Bozorgmehr.'
segments.s659-g240: چو again introduces a temporal clause 'when.' خورشید رخشنده 'the radiant sun' is described, and بد here qualifies that radiance as 'ill / ominous / unfavorable' (an adverbial/qualifying sense), with بر سپهر giving the location 'upon the sky.' The clause implies 'when the sun shone with an ominous aspect over the heavens.'
segments.s659-g401: "یکی" is an indefinite introducer meaning 'a certain' or 'there is one'; it heads the noun phrase. "نامور مایه‌دار" are two attributive adjectives placed after the indefinite noun (renowned, wealthy). "ایدرست" is an emphatic/adverbial element (archaic/poetic) meaning 'indeed' or 'truly', functioning like an emphatic copula that confirms the existence of such a person.
segments.s659-g402: The relative clause introduced by "که" ('that/who') modifies the preceding "یکی". "گنجش" = "گنج + ـش" is the noun 'treasure' with a third-person possessive suffix ('his/its treasure'). "ز" here is the poetic comparative preposition 'than/from' used in comparisons. "گنج" is the noun 'treasure' (the compared item), "تو" is the second-person pronoun 'your', and "افزون‌ترست" is the comparative predicate 'is greater/more abundant' (afzun-tar + -ast). The whole clause means 'whose treasure is greater than your treasure.'
