segments.s659-g403: "چنین" is an adverb 'thus/so' introducing the manner of the reply. "داد پاسخ" is a light-verb nominal construction where "پاسخ" (answer) is the noun and "داد" is the past tense verb 'gave' (i.e., 'gave an answer'). "که" begins the reported content. "آری" is the interjection 'yes', and "رواست" = "روا + ست" is an adjectival-predicate 'it is fitting/permissible' (the copula is fused with the adjective).
segments.s659-g404: This is a subordinate explanatory clause headed by "که" ('that/for'). "از" is the preposition 'from/out of'. "فره" (farr) is the technical Old/Iranian noun 'divine glory/splendour'. "پادشاهی ما" is the noun phrase 'our kingship/kingship of ours' (possessive pronoun follows the noun), and "ست" is the copula 'is'. Together: 'that it is from the divine glory of our kingship.'
segments.s659-g405: "دگر گفت" is a short narrative clause 'again he said' ("دگر" = 'again', "گفت" = past-tense 'said'). "کای" is the archaic vocative particle 'O' placed before a title; "شهریار بلند" is the vocative nominal 'lofty/high king' with the adjective after the noun. The whole segment is a vocative opening to reported speech.
segments.s659-g406: "انوشه بدی" is a personal name in two parts (here functioning as the subject). The following phrase "وز بدی" is an archaic/poetic construction expressing source or cause ('from/by the burden/weight of evil' — "وز" used here as an old-form linking word), and "بی‌گزند" is a predicative adjective 'unharmed / without injury'. Syntactically the name is the subject and the phrase "وز بدی" gives the ablative/cause for the state described by "بی‌گزند".
segments.s659-g407: "اسیران رومی" is the noun phrase 'Roman captives' (noun + ethnic adjective). The relative clause "که آورده‌اند" ('that they have brought') follows; "آورده‌اند" is the present perfect/pluperfect compound meaning 'they have brought' or 'they brought' (third-person plural with auxiliary).
segments.s659-g408: "بسی" is a quantifier meaning 'many/much' in classical usage. "شیرخواره" is the direct object 'suckling(s), infants'. "درو" is a contracted locative/compound ('در او' → 'there/in it' or 'from there' depending on context) and marks source/location. "برده‌اند" is the perfect compound 'they have carried off / they took away' (برد + اند). Together: 'they have carried off many sucklings from there.'
segments.s659-g681: کسی کاو کند آفرین بر جهان = a relative clause 'whoever / someone who praises the world'. کسی is the indefinite 'someone/whoever'. کاو here is an archaic poetic contraction (كَ او = 'که او') functioning as a relative marker 'who' linking to the verb کند. کند is the present/subjunctive/3rd‑person form of 'to do/make' used with آفرین (an acclamation/praise) as its object and بر + noun marks the target ('praise upon the world'). Word order is subject — relative marker — verb — object phrase, typical in Ferdowsi's elevated diction.
segments.s659-g682: به ما بازگردد درودش نهان = a jussive/subjunctive result/wish clause: 'may ... be returned to us'. به marks the indirect object 'to/for us' and ما is the first‑person plural object. بازگردد is 3rd‑person singular present subjunctive/jussive ('may he/it return/be returned'). درودش is 'his greeting' (درود + enclitic ـش) as the thing returned. نهان is an adjective/adverb 'hidden/secretly' modifying how the greeting is returned.
segments.s659-g683: چنین داد پاسخ که آری رواست = 'He gave such an answer, namely that ...'. چنین داد پاسخ is a free word order (چنین + perfective داد + noun پاسخ) meaning 'he answered thus'. که introduces the quoted/subordinate content. آری is an emphatic particle 'indeed/yes', روا is a predicate adjective 'fitting/permissible', and the copula ست ('است' contracted to ست) follows, forming 'آری رواست' = 'indeed it is fitting.'
segments.s659-g684: که تاج زمانه سر پادشاست = the subordinate clause giving the content of the answer. که = 'that'. تاج زمانه is a genitival phrase 'the crown of the age' (تاج + زمانه). سر پادشاست literally reads 'on the head is the monarch' or more idiomatically 'should be on the ruler's head'; سر 'head' follows تاج زمانه in predicative position and پادشاست (a fused form of 'پادشاه است' / 'is the ruler' or 'is of the ruler') expresses the relation—overall: 'that the crown of the age belongs on the ruler's head.'
segments.s659-g685: جهان را چنین شهریاران سرند = a topical fronting: جهان را marks 'the world' as the theme/affected domain, چنین 'thus/so' sets the manner, شهریاران is the nominative plural 'kings', and سرند is an archaic/poetic form indicating their relation to 'head' (here meaning 'they are heads' or 'they are the heads of it'). The clause states the role/status of kings with inverted, elevated word order common in epic verse.
segments.s659-g686: ازیرا چنین بر سران افسرند = explains consequence: ازیرا (archaic for از این رو / 'therefore') + چنین 'thus' introduces the result. بر سران 'upon the heads' uses بر + plural سران. افسرند here is the plural predicate formed from افسر (a helmet) meaning 'helmets are (placed) on [them]'—poetically it states that helmets sit upon the heads. The clause uses verbless or nominal predication where 'افسرند' functions as 'are helmets' or 'are placed as helmets'.
segments.s659-g687: گذشتم ز توقیع نوشین‌روان = 'I passed by/from the inscription of the sweet‑souled'. گذشتم is past tense first‑person singular 'I passed/went by'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/by'. توقیع نوشین‌روان is a genitival/attributive phrase: توقیع 'inscription/signed note' qualified by نوشین‌روان 'sweet‑souled' (an epithet); the whole phrase is the object/ground of گذشتم.
segments.s659-g688: جهان پیر و اندیشه من جوان = a verbless nominal clause with coordinated predicates: جهان پیر 'the world (is) old' and اندیشه من جوان 'and my thought (is) young.' Persian often omits the copula in present descriptions; here juxtaposition contrasts the aged world with the speaker's youthful mind. من is the patient/possessor 'my' attached to اندیشه.
segments.s659-g689: مرا طبع = 'my temperament/nature' uses مرا in the older possessive/dative sense (’my’/’to me’) before the noun طبع. نشگفت is the classical/poetic form of شگفت (from شگفتن) and here functions as a past-state verb meaning 'was astonished' (with the initial ن as negation in older usages or as part of the verbal form depending on context); اگر + تیز گشت (= 'if it became sharp') is the conditional clause ('if it became/grew keen'), where گشت is the past of گشتن used to mean 'became.' The clause order places the main result before the conditional, a common poetic inversion.
segments.s659-g690: به پیری = 'in/into old age' where به is the preposition marking temporal circumstance and پیری is 'old age.' چنین = 'thus/so' modifies آتش‌آمیز, an adjectival compound 'fire‑like' or 'fiery/ardent.' گشت again is the past of گشتن, here 'became/turned so' completing the predication 'it became so fiery.'
segments.s659-g691: ز منبر = 'from the pulpit' (ز is the poetic preposition 'from'); چو = 'when' introduces a temporal clause. محمود is the name being uttered, and گوید is the verb 'says/utters' (here reported in the simple present/past-poetic sense). خطیب (placed after the verb) is the agent 'the preacher/orator' — classical word order allows the verb before the subject.
segments.s659-g692: بدین = 'by this/with this' (ب as a prepositional prefix merging with دین here to give 'بدین' = 'by this'); محمد is the indirect object/toward‑whom, گراید is the verb 'turns/inclines' (present/past-poetic sense), and صلیب is the subject 'the cross.' The clause means that as a consequence of that proclamation the cross (symbolically) turns toward Muhammad.
segments.s659-g693: همی‌گفتم is the habitual/imperfect past 'I was saying' or 'I used to say.' این نامه را is the direct object 'this letter' with را marking definiteness/object. چند گاه = 'for a while/some time' (چند modifying گاه to indicate an indefinite short duration). The segment sets up a reported recommendation.
segments.s659-g694: نهان بد here functions as an instruction 'hide [it]' or 'make it hidden' — نهان is 'hidden/secret' and the following بد is an archaic/poetic verbal element forming an imperative-like expression (classical diction). ز خورشید و کیوان و ماه = 'from the sun and Kiwan (Saturn) and the moon,' where ز is the poetic 'from' and the list names celestial bodies to be kept from. In modern Persian one would expect a different verb form, but this is idiomatic poetic phrasing.
segments.s659-g695: چو = 'when'; تاج سخن = a genitive phrase 'the crown of speech/poetry' (تاج governed by سخن). نام محمود گشت = 'the name Mahmud became' — گشت is the past 'became,' so the clause says the name Mahmud was elevated to the crown of speech.
segments.s659-g696: ستایش = 'praise' (subject); به آفاق = 'to/throughout the horizons' (به marking direction/extent); موجود گشت = 'became present/existent' where موجود is 'present/existent' and گشت 'became,' so the clause means praise spread or became manifest across the realms.
segments.s659-g41: بدو (prepositional/adverbial) + گفت is a fronted dative/locative phrase meaning 'to him' or 'unto him' followed by the verb 'said' (گفت). کای is the vocative particle 'O', سگ is an insult 'dog/scoundrel', and تو را places 'you' with the enclitic را as emphasis/topic before the reported-clause introducer این‌که; the whole string functions as the reporting frame: 'he said to him, "O dog, you...".'
segments.s659-g42: که introduces the subordinate reported clause 'that...'. پالایش طبع is a genitive/compound noun 'purification/refinement of (one's) nature'. بتـوان is the potential/ability verb ('be able') in the classical/poetic form, and نهفت here functions as a participial/adjectival or passive notion 'to be hidden' — together meaning 'that the refinement of temperament can be hidden.'
segments.s659-g43: نه negates the following statement: 'نه من اورمزدم' = 'I am not Ahura Mazda.' وگر is an archaic conditional/contrastive connector ('and if' / 'or if') introducing the hypothetical 'بهمنم' = 'I am Bahman' (the -م ending marks first person), i.e. 'nor (am) Bahman' in context.
segments.s659-g44: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of'. خاکست = 'ashes'. وز is here the fused sequence و + ز ('and from'), linking another source; باد و آتش = 'wind and fire'. تنم is the substantive 'my body' (تن + possessive -م). The phrase states the speaker's corporeal nature: 'my body is of ash, and of wind and fire.'
segments.s659-g45: جهاندار 'the sovereign' is the subject. چندی here means 'for a while'. زبان رنجه کرد is an idiomatic verb phrase: زبان (speech/tongue) + رنجه کرد (past of کردن with رنجه 'to weary/trouble') — 'he wearied his tongue' = 'he spoke/exerted his tongue' (i.e. spoke at some length or with effort).
segments.s659-g46: ندید is the past negative 'he did not see'. ایچ پاسخ = 'any answer' with ایچ (nothing/not at all) emphasizing absence. جز introduces exclusion 'except', and باد سرد 'the cold wind' is the only 'reply' perceived — note ار here is an archaic connector whose specific sense in this idiom is not given in the provided list.
segments.s659-g47: بپژمرد is a past/inchoative verb 'withered/waned' (b- prefix + root). بر جای = 'on the spot/where he stood'. بوزرجمهر is the proper name 'Bozorgmehr' (the person affected). The clause describes his physical/emotional collapse on the spot.
segments.s659-g48: ز شاه و ز کردار is a causal phrase 'from the king and from the deeds/actions'. گردان سپهر is a poetic modifier: گردان 'revolving/turning' modifies سپهر 'the sky/heaven' — 'deeds that turn/sway the sky' (an epic epithet). Together they indicate the causes that struck Bozorgmehr.
segments.s659-g641: 'که' here introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that'. 'درد دل' is a fixed collocation meaning emotional pain/sorrow (literally 'pain of the heart'); 'مردم زیردست' = 'the people under (one's) hand', i.e. the subjects. In Persian poetry the ezāfe linking words is often omitted, so the phrase reads literally 'that [the] sorrow [of the] heart [of the] people under (him)'.
segments.s659-g642: 'نخواهد' is a third‑person negative future/volitional from خواستن (here 'will not [do/wish/attend to]'); it precedes the subject in this poetic order. 'جهاندار' is the subject 'sovereign' and 'یزدان‌پرست' is an appositional adjective 'God‑worshipping/pious'. The exact object of 'نخواهد' (e.g. 'heed' or 'care for') is implicit and supplied by the previous segment.
segments.s659-g643: 'پی' precedes the noun and here names the 'foot/base' (foundation) of the building; 'کاخ آباد' = 'the flourishing/prosperous palace'. 'را' marks the definite object and 'بر کنید' is an imperative (plural) of بر کردن; in this context بر کردن + پی means 'to pull up / demolish the foundation' — object before verb is normal in Persian imperatives and in poetry.
segments.s659-g644: 'به' is instrumental here ('with/by'), 'گل' is used in the sense 'mud/clay' (not 'flower'), and 'بام او را' 'his roof' is the definite object marked by 'را'. 'توانگر کنید' is an imperative (plural) 'make ... wealthy/rich' where توانگر is used as the resultive adjective — literally 'make his roof wealthy by (using) clay.'
segments.s659-g645: 'شود' is the third‑person singular of شدن used here as 'will become' (future/passive sense). 'کاخ ویران' is the predicate 'palace ruined'; 'تو را' functions as a possessive/topic marker 'of you' (poetic placement), and 'ز هرچ بود' literally 'from/whatever there was' — 'هرچ' = 'whatever' and 'بود' is past 'was', so the phrase says 'will become ruined from whatever of yours there was.'
segments.s659-g646: 'بماند' is a present/subjunctive/continuative form meaning 'remain' (here used as a future/resultative statement). 'پس از مرگ' = 'after death' (پس = 'after'), and the nouns 'نفرین و دود' ('curse and smoke') are what will be left; the verb stands before the nouns in this terse poetic order.
segments.s659-g647: 'ز' = 'from' (poetic), 'دیوان ما' = 'our register/record' (دیوان here = official register), 'نام او' = 'his name' as the definite object, and 'بسترید' is an imperative form of بستردن meaning 'to blot out/strike out/conceal'. The object precedes the verb, as is common in Persian poetry.
segments.s659-g648: This is a second imperative clause. 'بدر' = 'the full moon' and 'بر' is a prepositional element ('on/upon' or functioning to relate بدر to the following qualifier); 'چنو' = 'such/as such', 'را' marks the object, 'به‌کس' = 'to anyone'. The final verb 'مشمرید' is an archaic/obsolete form (uncertain in modern Persian) functioning here as an imperative meaning roughly 'do not set/esteem/offer (him) so to anyone' — i.e. do not exalt him before others as if he were the moon.
segments.s659-g665: This is a correlative pair presenting two alternatives: 'که ... به که ...' here functions like English 'whether ... or ...' (classical/poetic word order). 'که' introduces the option ('which/who/that'), 'بی‌دین' is a compound using the negative prefix بی‑ meaning 'without religion/impious', and 'بی‌پادشا' likewise 'without a king/kingless'. 'جهان' is the noun 'world' placed after بی‌دین in the classical order.
segments.s659-g666: خردمند باشد is a jussive/subjunctive expression: باشد here has the force 'let be / may be' (a literary optative) so 'let the wise be...'. برین is the preposition بر + demonstrative این (written poetically as ین) = 'upon this / to this', and بر گوا places the noun گوا ('witness') after the preposition for emphatic, poetic word order: 'as witness to this.'
segments.s659-g667: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed literary phrase 'he gave such an answer' (چنین = 'thus/so', داد = 'gave' as verb, پاسخ = 'answer'). که introduces the reported clause 'that...', and گفتم is the past first‑person ('I said') placed in the subordinate clause; همین ('this same/just that') is the content of the reply.
segments.s659-g668: شنید is simple past 'heard'. این سخن = 'this speech/these words' (این = 'this', سخن = 'speech/words'). مردم پاک‌دین is a noun phrase 'the devout people' (مردم = 'people', پاک‌دین = 'devout person/pious' used collectively). Word order is typical classical Persian: object first, verb after the subject phrase.
segments.s659-g669: This clause states a negative universal: جهان را ندید literally 'the world did not see' — an idiom meaning 'there has not been' or 'one has not seen' — followed by جهاندار بی‌دین 'a sovereign without religion/faith' (جهاندار = sovereign, بی‌دین = without religion). را is the definite/direct object marker attached to جهان.
segments.s659-g670: مگر introduces a concessive/exclusive clause here ('except that' / 'save that'). هر کسی = 'each/every person' (هر = every, کسی = person/one), دین دیگر گزید is a simple past clause 'chose another religion' (دین = religion, دیگر = other/another, گزید = chose). The whole line contrasts the absence of an irreligious sovereign with the fact that individuals adopt differing faiths.
segments.s659-g671: This is a balanced contrast: یکی ... و یکی ... 'one (person) ... and another ...' used distributively = 'some ... and some ...'. یکی = 'one/some', بت‌پرست = 'idolater', پاک‌دین = 'pious/devout'. The particle و simply links the contrasting types.
segments.s659-g672: یکی گفت = 'one said' (a speaker is introduced). نفرین به از آفرین is a compact comparative clause in classical order: 'نفرین ... به از ...' means 'a curse is preferable/ better than praise' — the structure 'X به از Y' is an older Persian comparative ('X is more than Y'). Here نفرین = 'curse', آفرین = 'praise/acclamation'.
segments.s659-g521: کسی is the indefinite subject 'someone/anyone'. کودهش is the possessed noun 'کوده + -ش' = 'his hut' (possessive -ش attached). کاست here is the noun 'کاه/کاست' meaning 'straw' (the material kept in the hut). باشد is a present/jussive form of 'to be' used in a conditional sense ('were/should be'); به کار is a prepositional phrase 'for use/for work' (به = 'for', کار = 'use/work'), completing the condition.
segments.s659-g522: بپوشد is the verb 'to clothe/cover' in a jussive/habitual sense ('would clothe/cover'). The omitted direct object is the person referred to by کسی in the previous segment; همه فره شهریار is a noun phrase 'all the (king's) farr' — همه modifies فره ('the whole/entire farr') and شهریار is a genitive modifier ('of the king'). The phrase means 'clothe [him] with the king's entire splendor'.
segments.s659-g523: دگر is an adverb meaning 'then/afterwards' (sequence). گفت is the verb 'said' (classical past); با introduces the addressee 'to/with' and هرکسی means 'everybody/each person' (the audience). پادشا is the noun 'king' and here is the topic of what was said ('he said to everyone [that] the king ...').
segments.s659-g524: This is a predicative clause: بزرگ است = 'is great' (بزرگ adjective + copula است). The coordinating و join the adjectives: بخشنده 'generous' and پارسا 'pious/virtuous'. The verb است applies to the whole series of predicates ('is great, generous, and pious').
segments.s659-g525: پرستار دیرینه مهرک is a nominal phrase 'Mehrak's long-standing caretaker' (پرستار = caretaker/watcher, دیرینه = long-standing/old, مهرک = proper name). چه کرد is the direct question 'what did [he] do?' with چه functioning as the interrogative and کرد the past-tense verb 'did'.
segments.s659-g526: که introduces the result clause 'that' (here: 'that his ...'). روزیش uses the root روز with the possessive -ش, here meaning 'his daily provision / sustenance' (classical usage روزی = livelihood). اندک شد is a compound meaning 'became small/meager' (اندک = little; شد = became). و joins the second result روی‌زرد 'pale-faced' (i.e. he grew pale).
segments.s659-g527: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'gave such an answer' (چنین = thus/so, داد = gave, پاسخ = answer). که introduces the content clause 'that ...'. او شد درشت = 'he became درشت' where درشت here means 'coarse/rude/insolent' — 'he became rude/harsh'.
segments.s659-g528: بران is an adverb/connector 'thereupon/thereon' ('on that'), کرده is the participle 'having done' (here part of a periphrastic sequence), خویش ش = 'his own disposition' (خوی + -ش possessive), and بنهاد پشت literally 'placed his back' — an idiom meaning 'he turned his back' / 'he withdrew/turned away'. The cluster describes the resulting action of turning away in pride or rudeness.
segments.s659-g385: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when' introducing a subordinate clause; بیمار زارست is a small clause where زارست = زار + ـست (old/poetic 'is wretched'), so 'the patient is wretched'. و links that clause to the following independent phrase; ما چون پزشک literally 'we as/like a physician' (چون = 'like') and places the simile before the noun it modifies.
segments.s659-g386: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of' (here 'from/averse to'), followed by دارو 'medicine'; گریزان is an adjectival/participle form 'fleeing/avoiding' modifying the understood subject ما. و simply coordinates the next phrase; ریزان is another participial adjective 'shedding/scattering' that precedes and modifies سرشک 'teardrops' (a poetic collocation: 'shedding teardrops').
segments.s659-g387: به یک دارو = 'by/with a single remedy' (به + numeral + noun). ار is the archaic conditional 'if'. او is the subject 'he'; ننگردد here means 'does not become / is not turned into' (classical present/future sense), and درست is the predicative adjective 'well/right/cured' following the verb (درست شدن = 'to be cured').
segments.s659-g388: زوان is an older spelling/word for 'tongue / speech' (i.e. 'talk'); از پزشکی is a prepositional phrase 'about/of medicine'. نخواهیم شست is the negative future 'we will not wash/clean/wipe' (نخواهیم = 'we will not/desire not to'); here شستن the tongue 'to wash the tongue from X' is an idiom meaning 'to refrain from speaking of X', so the clause means 'we will not refrain from speaking about medicine.' Word order places the object/topic (زوان / از پزشکی) before the verb phrase, typical in Persian.
segments.s659-g389: دگر = 'another' (an alternative/other person). موبدی is the agent 'mobed' (Zoroastrian priest) and is the subject of گفت; گفت is the past verb 'said' (here introducing reported speech). انوشه is a proper name (object of the following verb), and بدی in classical usage here functions as the 2nd-person past 'you gave' (an archaic verbal form) — so the line is introducing what the mobed said about giving to Anousheh.
segments.s659-g390: به marks the dative/benefactive 'to/for'; داد و دهش is a paired noun phrase 'justice (داد) and gift/grant (دهش)' (here items given). نیز = 'also/likewise'. توشه = 'provisions' or 'store' (what one gives for a journey). بدی repeats the archaic verbal form 'you gave' (so the phrase states that he gave justice and gifts and likewise provisions). The construction lists what was given, with the verb (بدی) applying to the whole list.
segments.s659-g391: سپهدار is a title 'commander'; گرگان here is the place-name 'Gorgan' used in apposition (commander of Gorgan). برفت is an archaic/perfect-type past verb 'went forth/left (his place)'; از نهفت = 'from hiding' where نهفت is a past-participle/nominal 'that which was hidden' (i.e. a hiding place). The clause describes emergence from concealment.
segments.s659-g392: به بیشه درآمد = 'entered into the grove/thicket' (به + locative noun + درآمد where درآمد functions as a verbal perfect 'came in/entered'). زمانی is an adverb 'for a time/for a while'; بخفت is the past tense 'he slept' (بخفتن = to sleep, classical form). The sequence gives successive actions: entered the thicket and then slept for a while.
segments.s659-g505: که is the subordinating particle introducing the clause ('that / that it is such that'). بی‌لشکر is a prefixed negative phrase meaning 'without an army' (بی- + لشکر). گشن is an adjectival/nominal description 'hungry' applied to the (understood) subject; Persian often omits the subject when it is clear from context. بیرون شود places the verb (شود, the classical/subjunctive/present form of شدن) at the end: here شدّن means 'go/become' so بیرون شود = 'goes out / should go out' (a hortative/optative or resultive sense in poetic narrative).
segments.s659-g506: دل دوستداران is a genitive construction 'the heart(s) of the admirers/supporters' (noun + plural modifier). پر از خون شود is a predicate in which پر 'full (of)' takes an of-phrase (از خون) and the copular/auxiliary شود (شدن) comes at the end: 'becomes full of blood' (a resultive statement; classical Persian places the verb last).
segments.s659-g507: مگر here functions as an exception/conditional 'unless'. دشمنی is the noun 'enmity / hostile force' used as 'an enemy'. بد is an attributive adjective 'evil / bad / hostile', and سگالد is a verbal/adjectival form meaning 'one who sets dogs on / who assails with hounds' (a poetic compound describing a harrying attacker). بدوی is an adjective/adverbial intensifier; in classical usage it conveys a rough, savage or primitive quality, here characterizing the enemy.
segments.s659-g508: بیاید is the present/subjunctive/optative form of آمدن 'to come' (here 'should come / comes'). به چاره literally 'to (a) remedy' = 'seeking a solution'; Persian uses به + noun to indicate purpose or direction. بنالد is the present/subjunctive/habitual form of نالیدن 'to lament, to moan' (here 'he will lament' or 'he will cry out'). The final بدوی again characterizes the manner (poetically 'in a brutal/savage way'); word order places the verb at the end of the clause, as is typical in Ferdowsi's style.
segments.s659-g509: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'he gave such an answer' (چنین 'thus' + داد 'gave' + پاسخ 'answer'); note that داد here is a verb 'gave' not the noun 'justice'. که introduces the content clause 'that ...'. In the clause داد و خرد, داد is a noun 'justice' paired with خرد 'wisdom' (a noun + و + noun coordination), so the second occurrence of داد is nominal rather than verbal.
segments.s659-g510: تن پادشا را is a marked object: تن 'body/person' + پادشا 'king' with the object marker را. همی‌پرورد is the present/habitual form of پروردن 'to nourish, foster', with the archaic continuous prefix همی- (common in Ferdowsi) giving 'nurtures/sustains'. The verb at the clause end governs the preceding noun phrase: 'justice and wisdom nurture the king's person.'
segments.s659-g511: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. دادگر is a noun/adjective 'a just (ruler)' — in Persian compounds like this the adjective-noun order is flexible. چند here functions adverbially 'somewhat / a little' (classical Persian often uses چند to mean 'a bit' or 'some'). بی‌کس 'without companions / alone' is a prefixed negative construction, and بود is the past/copular verb 'was' (in this conditional context marking hypothetical past or general condition).
segments.s659-g512: ورا is a clitic/old form meaning 'for him / to him'. پاسبان 'guard' is the noun; راستی here denotes 'uprightness / rectitude' (an abstract moral quality) and is the thing serving as پاسبان. بس is the predicate adjective/word 'enough / sufficient', and بود again is the copula 'was'. Word order places the predicate at the end: 'uprightness as a guard was enough for him.'
segments.s659-g649: دگر گفت = 'another (one) said' — دگر (another) comes first as the topic and گفت is the past tense 'said'. کای شاه فرخ نژاد is a vocative phrase: کای is a classical vocative/title ('O' or Kay), شاه is 'king', and فرخ نژاد is an epithet 'fortunate-born' (adjective + نژاد 'of lineage'). The whole phrase addresses the king directly after the clause 'another said'.
segments.s659-g650: بسی‌گیری از جم و کاوس یاد is an exhortation. بسی‌گیری (a nominalized or verbal phrase: 'take/hold much' or 'great remembrance') governs از + names: 'from/of Jam and Kāvus'. یاد is 'remembrance/memory' and completes the object. Word order is typical Persian: (verb/noun phrase) + از + names + object.
segments.s659-g651: بدان گفت is an older/formal phrasing meaning 'he said to him' (بدان = be + ān/that/to him). تا introduces a purpose/result clause here ('so that'). از پس = 'after', and مرگ من = 'my death' (possessive order: noun + من).
segments.s659-g652: نگردد is a negative jussive/optative sense 'may not become / shall not turn into' (here 'become hidden'). نهان is the predicate adjective 'hidden'. افسر و ترگ من are the coordinated objects 'my helmet and my rag/garment' marked by the second من. The verb precedes the adjective and the objects follow, a common poetic order.
segments.s659-g653: دگر گفت again means 'another said'. کز here is a classical particle 'to/that/who' introducing the addressee or the referent; in this line it connects the speech to بهمن سرفراز ('Bahman the proud'). سرفراز is an epithet after the name.
segments.s659-g654: چرا is the interrogative 'why' fronted for emphasis. شاه ایران is the subject 'the King of Iran' and بپوشید is the past tense/past-subjunctive form of پوشیدن used here with the meaning 'to cover/conceal' (sense 'to cover; conceal'). راز is the direct object 'the secret'. The clause asks why the king concealed the secret.
segments.s659-g655: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'thus he gave answer' (چنین 'thus', داد 'gave' = past of دادن, پاسخ 'answer'). که introduces the content clause. او را is the definite object 'him' (را marking a definite object), and خرد 'reason/wisdom' is the grammatical subject that will be linked to the verb in the next segment.
segments.s659-g656: بپیچد is a subjunctive/jussive form of پیچیدن 'to twist/turn' meaning 'would twist' or 'turn'. همی is an adverbial particle here ('continually/indeed') placed after the verb for emphasis. وز هوا ('a blast from the air') treats وز as 'gust/blast' and هوا as 'desire/passion' in poetic sense; برخورد is the impact/strike (past/nominalized action) — i.e., a gust struck (him). Together they explain that desire (a wind) struck and so his reason was twisted.
segments.s659-g321: سپاس is a noun meaning 'praise/thanks' and begins a formula of blessing. از is the preposition linking the praise to its addressee (here 'to/for' God). خداوند خورشید و ماه is a noun phrase where خداوند is the head ('the Lord') and خورشید و ماه are coordinated modifiers/appositives ('of the sun and the moon').
segments.s659-g322: روان را is the definite object/topic ('the soul') marked by را. به دانش is a prepositional phrase 'by/through knowledge' that modifies the following predication. نماینده is an agentive/participial noun 'one who shows/represents' or 'guide' and here takes راه ('the way') as its complement: '(who) made [the soul] a guide to the way.' segments.s659-g323: نداند is the classical third-person negative form of 'to know' ('knows not'). جز او is an exclu­sive phrase 'except him'. آشکارا و راز are two coordinated nouns/adverbial qualities ('the manifest/evident and the secret'), serving as the things that are not known by others — they are objects/complements of نداند.

segments.s659-g325: سه is the numeral 'three' modifying a following descriptive phrase. درست رخشان is a compound adjectival description ('true/perfect and shining/radiant') where adjectives follow the numeral. به درج اندرون is a locative/prepositional phrase: به here = 'in/on/at' and درج اندرون = 'the inner inscription/inner recess' indicating where these three shining things are placed.
segments.s659-g326: غلافش is the noun 'its sheath' with the third-person possessive suffix. بود is the past copula 'was'. ز آنچ گفتم برون is a prepositional phrase 'from/according to that which I said outside' — ز = 'from/because of', آنچ = 'that which' (a nominalized relative), گفتم here refers back to the speaker's earlier words, and برون 'out' or 'outside' closes the phrase. segments.s659-g327: یکی and دیگری are contrasted indefinite pronouns 'one' and 'the other'. سفته here functions as a descriptive term applied to the first item (classical diction often uses nouns adjectivally). نیم سفت is a compound adjective 'half‑hard' describing the second item. segments.s659-g328: دگر 'another/other' begins the phrase; آنک is a classical relative pronoun 'that which/which'. آهن is the noun 'iron'. ندیده‌ست is a perfective construction 'has not seen' (ندیده + است contracted), third person singular. جفت is predicate/nominal complement meaning 'match' or 'pair'; together the clause says that this other is such that iron has not seen its match. segments.s659-g65: بپرسید is a narrative past verb with the archaic b‑ prefix (here: ‘he asked’). یک روز is a temporal adverbial (“one day”) placed after the verb in inverted/narrative word order. بوزرجمهر is the subject/name following the time expression.
segments.s659-g66: ز introduces the person being addressed or the source (‘from/of’); پرورده شاه is a genitive phrase ‘the one reared by the king’ (پرورده = nurtured/fosterling). خورشید چهر is an epithet meaning literally ‘sun‑faced’ (خورشید modifying چهر = face), placed after the noun as a poetic attributive.
segments.s659-g67: که introduces a subordinate content clause ('that' / introducing what is to be taught). او را uses را as the definite object marker meaning ‘him’. پرستش is the noun ‘worship’ and together with چون کنی ('how you do') forms an indirect question/complement: همی is an archaic/progressive particle often marking continuous or emphatic action ('as/indeed/so'), چون here means ‘how’, and کنی is the present/subjunctive 2nd‑person form ‘you do/perform’ (i.e. ‘how you worship him’).
segments.s659-g68: بیاموز is an imperative form (here used as a request/command ‘teach’ or ‘instruct me’ in classical usage). تا introduces a purpose/result clause ('so that / in order that'). کوشش افزون کنی: کوشش 'effort', افزون 'greater/more', and کنی is the subjunctive 'you may do/make' (purpose: 'so that you may increase your effort').
segments.s659-g69: پرستنده is the speaker ('the worshipper'). گفت is the narrative past 'said' (classical usage). ای is the vocative particle 'O', and سر موبدان is a vocative phrase 'head/chief of the mobeds' (سر = chief/leader; موبدان = mobeds, Zoroastrian priests).
segments.s659-g70: چنان دان is an imperative/instructional phrase 'consider/know it thus' (چنان = thus, دان = know). که introduces the content clause: امروز 'today', شاه 'the king', and ردان is a predicative/active form meaning 'expelling / driving away' (here: 'will drive out' or 'is about to drive away').
segments.s659-g71: چو از خوان برفت is a temporal subordinate clause 'when he left/rose from the table' (چو = when; از خوان = from the banquet/table; برفت is the past form 'he went/left' using the classical prefix). آب بگساردم is the main clause: آب 'water' and بگساردم is a first‑person past form meaning 'I let loose/released/poured' (i.e. 'I caused water to flow').
segments.s659-g72: زمین is the subject 'the earth'. ز آبدستان is an ablative/source phrase 'from the water‑bearer' (آبدستان = water‑attendant/cupbearer). مگر here functions in an exclusive/negative sense ('except/only' or leading to a negative result) and یافت is past 'found/obtained'; نم is 'moisture/dew' — together meaning the earth could not obtain any moisture from the water‑bearer.
segments.s659-g489: چنین داد پاسخ که is a fixed narrative formula: چنین modifies داد/پاسخ (“gave such an answer”). داد is the past tense of دادن (“gave”), پاسخ is the noun “answer,” and که introduces the content clause that follows. او را uses را as a topical/definite marker (“as for him” / “him”), and ز + noun (ز آز) here marks the relation “from/with respect to greed.”
segments.s659-g490: کمر بر میان است is a locative/copular clause: کمر ‘waist/girdle’ is the subject, بر میان is a locative phrase “on/about the middle,” and است is the copula ‘is.’ دور از نیاز is a simple adjectival phrase ‘far from need’ (دور + از + noun). The word order is typical poetic Persian (subject – locative – copula – predicative phrase).
segments.s659-g491: کسی را گزینید is an imperative construction: کسی را ‘someone’ with را marking the (definite/selected) object, and گزینید is the plural or polite imperative ‘choose!’ (here addressing a ruler/advisor). کز introduces a relative clause ‘who/that,’ and رنج خویش is ‘his own hardship’ (رنج = hardship/suffering; خویش = possessive clitic ‘his’ formed from خوی + ش).
segments.s659-g492: به‌پرهیز is an exhortative/imperative form meaning ‘refrain/avoid’ (the prefix به- here joins the verb for euphony). و simply connects the two clauses. باشدش is an optative/putative expression ‘let it be to him’ or ‘may it be his’ (باشد + clitic ش). گنج خویش = ‘his treasure/wealth’ (گنج = treasure; خویش = ‘his’), so the clause wishes that the chosen man’s treasure remain his own.
segments.s659-g493: This is a descriptive noun phrase: جهاندیده (compound adjective ‘worldly‑wise’) precedes مردی (‘a man’). The adjectives درشت و درست follow and coordinate (‘stout/rough and proper/upright’). The order (adjective – noun – adjectival modifiers) is a poetic arrangement emphasizing the qualities.
segments.s659-g494: که introduces a relative clause ‘who/that.’ او is the clause subject ‘he.’ رای here means ‘counsel/opinion’ and is the object; درویش (‘dervish, poor ascetic’) modifies رای (the counsel of the dervish). سازد is 3rd‑person singular present/simple tense of ساختن ‘to make/form’ (‘makes/sets’), and نخست ‘first’ is an adverbial qualifier at the end (“first”).
segments.s659-g495: یکی گفت is a short narrative formula ‘one (someone) said’; here یکی is indefinite ‘someone/a certain one’ and گفت is the verbal/nominal form used in narration. سالار خوالیگران is a noun phrase ‘the commander/chief of the خوالیگران’ (خوالیگران is an obscure/rare group-name in the text; سالار is the head/title).
segments.s659-g496: همی‌نالد is an archaic/poetic present form: the prefix همی‑ marks continuous/habitual aspect and نالد is from نالیدن ‘to moan/complain’ (‘he laments’). از here means ‘about/concerning’ (complains about X). شاه is ‘the king.’ وز and مهتران form the remainder ‘and the chiefs/notables’ in meaning, but وز is an archaic conjunction/connector in classical usage and مهتران (‘chiefs’) is a plural; both are literary/archaic items typical of Ferdowsi’s diction.
segments.s659-g57: بفرمود is an honorific past verb 'he ordered/said'. تا introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that/so that' (purpose or reported command). روی سندان is a prepositional phrase ('on/upon the anvil') placed before the verb; کنند is 3rd-person plural present-subjunctive/jussive used in subordinate clauses after a past command (here 'they should/should make').
segments.s659-g58: بداننده is the noun 'the knower' (here the one to be acted on) used without the object marker را (typical in classical/poetic Persian). بر کاخ زندان is a locative/goal phrase 'a prison upon/in the palace' (بر = on/upon + کاخ = palace; زندان کنند = 'they make a prison' → 'they imprison'). کننـد again is the 3rd‑plural subjunctive used in the commanded subordinate clause.
segments.s659-g59: دران کاخ is the fused classical form of در آن کاخ 'in that palace' (در + آن). بنشست is the past tense 'he sat' / 'took his seat'. The proper name بوزرجمهر follows the verb in this poetic order; he is the subject who sat in the palace.
segments.s659-g60: ازو is a prepositional pronoun 'from/of him'. برگسسته is a past‑participle/adjectival form describing a state relative to ازو (literally 'turned away/estranged from him') and modifies the following noun phrase; it functions attributively. جهاندار مهر is the title + name 'the sovereign/world‑ruler Mehr' — together the phrase means 'Mehr the sovereign', qualified by برگسسته.
segments.s659-g61: یکی here is an indefinite 'one (a certain one)'. خویش is 'of his own/one of his kin' (kinship), and بودش is the past 'there was to him / he had' with the enclitic -ش referring back to the possessor; the predicate دلیر و جوان follow and describe that person ('brave and young').
segments.s659-g62: پرستنده is an agent noun 'devotee/faithful follower' and here describes the same person; شاه is 'the king' governed by پرستنده (i.e. 'follower of the king'). نوشین‌روان is an epithet 'sweet‑souled' placed after the noun as a descriptive adjective — a typical Persian epithet order.
segments.s659-g63: بهرجای is an adverb 'everywhere' or 'in every place' placed up front for emphasis. با شاه در کاخ are prepositional phrases 'with the king in the palace' and بود is the past copula 'was' (third singular). The order (adverb + prepositional phrases + verb) is typical in poetic/classical Persian.
segments.s659-g64: به گفتار is a prepositional phrase 'in speech' or 'in manner of speaking' (به + گفتار). با شاه 'toward/with the king' modifies the scope of the next adjective; گستاخ is the adjective 'impudent/audacious'. بود is again the past copula 'was' — together: 'in speech he was impudent with the king'.
segments.s659-g697: زمانه (subject) + بنام وی (prepositional phrase) + آباد باد (predicate with optative). بنام is the fused preposition بِ + نام meaning "in the name of/for the sake of"; وی is the poetic third‑person pronoun "him." آباد is an adjective "prosperous," and باد is the optative particle "may (it be)" forming the wish "may X be prosperous." segments.s659-g698: سپهر is the subject "heavens/sky," followed by the phrase از سر تاج او giving the reason or locus (از here = "because of / on account of / from the top of"); سر is used in the sense "top/summit" ("the top of his crown"). شاد is the predicate adjective "glad/happy," and باد is again the optative "may (it be)." segments.s659-g699: جهان is the noun "the world," followed by the verb بستد (third‑person past, here "shut/closed/ended/put an end to"). از introduces the group being affected: بت‌پرستان هند "the idol‑worshippers of India." The verb بستد is used in the perfective/past sense and the object phrase follows it; the construction is somewhat compressed/poetic.
segments.s659-g700: به تیغی uses به as an instrumental/agent marker "by/with a sword." تیغی is an indefinite noun "a sword." که دارد is a relative clause "which he has/possesses" with دارد in the present used descriptively. چو introduces a simile "as/like," and پرند is an archaic/poetic form meaning "they fly" (present plural of پریدن) — so "like that, the Romans fly." segments.s659-g673: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/by' introducing the cause. گفتار is the noun 'speech' (the cause). ویران is an adjective 'ruined' that links to the verb نگردد, which is the negative third-person singular of گشتن/گردیدن meaning 'become' (here 'will/become ruined'). جهان is the subject placed after the verb for emphasis: 'From speech the world does not become ruined.'
segments.s659-g674: بگو is the imperative 'say' addressing someone. آنچ is the relative/indefinite 'that which'. رایت is an archaic/borrowed perfect 'you saw' (رأیت) rather than the noun 'banner', and بود is the past auxiliary 'was' forming a past statement ('you had seen' / 'you saw'). در نهان is an adverbial phrase 'in secret/hidden'. segments.s659-g675: هرآنگه که is a temporal/subordinating phrase 'whenever/when'. شد is the past/preterite third-person singular of شدن 'became'. تخت is the noun 'throne' and بی‌پادشا is a postpositive adjective 'kingless' (literally 'without a king'), so the clause reads 'whenever the throne became kingless.'
segments.s659-g676: خردمندی and دین form a coordinated compound subject 'wisdom and religion'. نیارد is an older negative third-person singular verb (classically spelled نیاورد/نِیارد) meaning 'does not bring / does not receive' and takes بها ('value/honor') as its object. segments.s659-g677: یکی introduces an indefinite speaker 'someone/a certain one'. گفت here is the past verb 'said' introducing direct speech (the supplied dictionary senses were for 'conversation', so the exact past-verb sense is used). کای is the vocative particle 'O', followed by شاه 'king' and خرم 'joyful' as a vocative phrase. نهان appears at the end of this segment as an adverb 'secretly', anticipating the verb in the next segment.
segments.s659-g678: سخن is the object 'speech/words'. راندی is a past second-person form (–ی ending) of راندن used idiomatically here 'you uttered / you drove forth (words)'. چند پیش is an adverbial phrase 'a little while ago' or 'some time ago' (چند = 'a few/some', پیش = 'before/ago' or 'in the presence of'), and مهان is the plural or collective 'guests/visitors', so the phrase means 'you spoke words in secret a little while ago before the guests.'
segments.s659-g679: یکی again introduces a speaker ('one'). آنکه is the relative 'he/that which (which)'. گفتی here functions as 'you said / you mentioned' (second-person past), so آنکه گفتی = 'the one you mentioned'. زمانه is used as 'Time' or 'Fortune' (a personified fate), and منم is the emphatic copula 'I am'. segments.s659-g680: بد و نیک is a coordinated pair 'bad and good' (ill and good). او را uses the object/possessive marker را after the pronoun 'he/him' to mark the affected party ('his'). بهانه is a predicate noun 'pretext / excuse / cause', and منم 'I am' closes the clause: 'I am his pretext/cause for bad and good.' segments.s659-g473: چنین گفت is a formula 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus'; گفت = 3rd‑person past 'said'). کای is an archaic vocative 'O'; مرد گردن‌فراز is the noun plus attributive compound 'man, proud‑necked' addressed by the speaker. segments.s659-g474: چنین here functions as 'such' (modifying لشکری). لشکری 'an army/host' is the noun; گشن و وزین are coordinate adjectives ('hungry' = eager, and 'weighty/formidable'); گونه 'kind/manner' specifies 'of this kind.' ساز is an imperative 'make/raise' (poetic short form of ساختن), ordering the formation of that army.
segments.s659-g475: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. برگاشت is a past verb here meaning 'turned'/'gave (his) back' (poetic use of برگرفتن/برگاشتن), او is the subject 'he', and پشت بر شهریار is the object phrase 'his back toward the king' (پشت = 'back', بر = 'on/toward', شهریار = 'the king'). segments.s659-g476: نبیند is a present (classical present/subjunctive form with negative prefix n-) used here to mean 'no one sees' or 'is not seen by anyone'. کس follows the verb as an indefinite pronoun ('anyone' but rendered negative by ن- → 'no one'), او را is the accusative 'him' (را marks the definite object), and بدین روزگار is a prepositional phrase 'in this time/age' (ب = prepositional prefix + دین here as part of بدین).
segments.s659-g477: به‌توقیع is an instrumental/agentive prepositional phrase 'by/with a signature; by decree'. گفت is 'he said' (reporting verb). آنک introduces the content clause 'that/which', and گردان سپهر is a poetic compound 'the revolving/turning sky' (گردان = 'revolving' modifying سپهر = 'heaven/sky').
segments.s659-g478: گشاده‌ست is the copular predicate 'is open/revealed'. با رای او is an ablative/instrumental phrase 'by his counsel/plan' (با = 'with/by', رای = 'counsel/plan', او = 'his'). چهر و مهر ('face and favour/affection') form the coordinated theme whose openness is asserted; together they mean his countenance and favour are laid open/revealed.
segments.s659-g479: به introduces a beneficiary/instrumental phrase 'with/regarding'. برزین is a proper name; سالار is his title 'commander'. و گنج و سپاه coordinate additional instrumental or accompanying nouns 'and treasure and army'. segments.s659-g480: نگردد is a negative present/subjunctive 'will not become'/'does not turn into'. تباه is the predicate adjective 'ruined/corrupt'. اختر هور و ماه is the coordinated subject/theme 'the star (splendour) Hoor and the moon' (اختر = 'star'; هور here = 'splendour' or a poetic name; ماه = 'moon'), so the clause states these will not be ruined.
segments.s659-g329: چو + بشنید is a temporal clause: چو here means 'when' and بشنید is 3rd‑person past 'he heard.' دانای رومی is the subject ('the wise man of Rûm/the Roman sage') placed after the temporal clause; کلید is the direct object that will be brought by that subject.
segments.s659-g330: بیاورد is the main verb (3rd‑person past 'he brought/produced') whose subject is دانای رومی from the previous segment. و links a second verb; نوشین‌روان is the object of بنگرید, and بنгرید (past form with the old literary prefix ب‑) means 'he looked' or 'he gazed (at)'.
segments.s659-g331: نهفته is a predicate/adjectival past participle 'hidden' placed clause‑initial for emphasis. یکی حقه بد is an indefinite noun phrase 'a certain bad/evil trick' (یکی = 'a certain', حقه = 'trick'), and در میان 'in the middle / amid' gives its location.
segments.s659-g332: به here marks location/into ('in/into'); حقه is used as a container (the sleight‑of‑hand cup/pouch) and درون reinforces 'inside of.' پرده پرنیان is a genitival phrase 'a silken curtain' (پرنیان = silken). segments.s659-g333: سه گوهر is the subject 'three gems.' بدان here is the prepositional phrase meaning 'in that' (formed from ب‑ + an archaic demonstrative), followed by حقه 'pouch'; اندر is another locative preposition 'within,' and نهفت is the past verbal/adjectival form 'were hidden/lay concealed.'
segments.s659-g334: چنان هم که is a connective phrase 'just as' or 'in the same way that'; که introduces the subordinate clause. دانای ایران is 'the wise man of Iran' (the Iranian sage) and بگفت is the past 'he said/told,' so the clause states that the discovery matches what that sage had said.
segments.s659-g335: نخستین ز گوهر = 'the first of the gems.' یکی سفته بود is a nominal predicate 'was one safteh' (سفته here = a safteh, a written pledge/pledge‑token), with بود as the past copula concluding the clause.
segments.s659-g336: دوم begins a new clause 'the second (was)…' with the copula omitted (common in Persian); نیم سفت is an adjectival description 'half‑hard / semi‑solid.' و links the final item; سیم نابسود is a noun + adjective 'silver (which is) unspoiled/unconsumed' (نابسود here describes the silver), giving the third gem's character.
segments.s659-g393: بنه برد is a past action: بنه (object) precedes the verb برد (here a past ‘took/gained’). از گیل is a prepositional phrase modifying برد ('from the soil/from the clay' — گیل is poetic here). The clause ends with و او برهنه where the copula is omitted: او برهنه = 'he (is) naked'.
segments.s659-g394: همی‌بازگردد is the present/habitual form with the prefix همی (continuous/habitual) + بازگردد ('returns/comes back'), so 'keeps returning'. ز بهر is a poetic two-word phrase meaning 'for the sake of' or 'because of', and بنه is the object/reason ('for the pistachio').
segments.s659-g395: به توقیع is a prepositional phrase 'with/by means of a signature/written order'; پاسخ چنین داد is literally 'gave an answer thus' where پاسخ is the object and داد is the past verb 'gave/sent'. باز after داد here functions as 'back/again' (i.e. 'sent back / returned'). segments.s659-g396: که introduces reported content 'that…'. هستیم is first-person plural 'we are'. ازان (archaic/poetic = از آن) combines with لشکری to form 'that troop/force' or 'of that army', and بی‌نیاز is a predicate adjective 'without need/self-sufficient' describing the troop. The copula 'are' links to the predicate (هستیم … بی‌نیاز).
segments.s659-g397: کجا begins a rhetorical question 'where/how (can)'. پاسپانی کند is the verbal phrase 'does guard / keeps watch' (پاسپانی = guarding; کند = 3rd person present). بر سپاه is the prepositional complement 'over the army'. The clause asks how he could perform that duty.
segments.s659-g398: ز here marks cause 'because of' (poetic). بد خویشتن = 'his badness / bad character / ill disposition' (خویشتن = himself). را is the object/definite marker emphasizing خویشتن. ندارد نگاه literally 'has no look' = 'does not keep watch/has no care' — ندارد is the negated present 'does not have' and نگاه = 'gaze/attention'.
